author: Chris Jordan
body:
I’ve had a very poor experience with ScottishPower over the past week. I was left without heating for around a week due to ongoing meter issues and delays, with unclear and inconsistent information from customer service throughout.

Despite multiple contacts, I was given vague responses and no clear explanation of what was happening with my account or when it would be resolved. Communication has been frustrating and unhelpful, and I had to repeatedly chase for updates.

I have now raised a formal complaint, but the overall service and handling of the situation has caused significant inconvenience, wasted time, and unnecessary stress.
insight: Customer experienced a week-long heating outage due to meter issues compounded by poor communication and vague responses from customer service.

insight: Customer praised operator for professional, empathetic handling of a disability-related issue that prevented stress-induced seizures.

author: Hope Ndamati
body:
My experience with Scottish Power has been generally positive. Setting up my account was straightforward, and I found the online system easy to use for managing bills and submitting meter readings. The billing has been mostly accurate, and I appreciate the range of options available for payments, which makes it convenient to stay on top of things.
One thing they do well is communication—updates about tariffs, energy usage, and account changes are usually clear and helpful. I also found their customer service team polite and willing to assist when I had queries, which made a difference.
That said, there is still room for improvement, particularly with response times during busy periods, as it can sometimes take longer than expected to get through or receive follow-up.
Overall, Scottish Power provides a reliable service with good digital tools and helpful support, and I would recommend them based on my experience so far.
insight: User praises Scottish Power's digital tools, billing accuracy, and customer service, though notes room for improvement in response times during peak periods.

author: Simon
body: Since signing up in August 2025, I have been asking for a prepayment meter to be fitted for my electric… I was told that this could be done as soon as they took over the supply, which they did on the 13th August 2025. After numerous requests they eventually arranged an appointment to install a new compatible meter on the 27th November 2025… the engineer never arrived after waiting in all day!! I phoned Scottish power on the 28th November and was told they would look into it and someone would call me back, they never did!! Eventually they started calling regarding arrears on my account, which let’s face it, wouldn’t have occurred if they’d installed the prepayment meter when I asked!! Eventually I got a call back on the 27th December, saying that they had a problem their end and someone would call me in the 5th or 6th January… again no call received… I’d had enough at this point and just thought oh well I give up… oh just to state, my dd had been set up at £134.36, even though according to their app I was using approximately £50-60 per month for gas and electric in a 1 bed flat!! Another reason I wanted to pay as you go!! Anyway, I begrudgingly called them again in early March 2026, to find out what was happening, and got through to a line manager in customer service…. Very apologetic, and assured me my new meter would be fitted in the 30th March 2026….. and low and behold, almost in may now and still hasn’t been done, however they have reduced my dd to £92.36…. Disgusting service, considering I registered for their priority service due to depression and anxiety, and can’t deal with the stress of it all… then to top it off, I get letters from their debt collection, saying they will apply for a warrant if I don’t bring the arrears upto date, and guess what the warrant is for?… the fitment of a prepayment meter!!! You couldn’t make it up!! Useless company, and if I could give them less than one star I would!!
insight: Customer experienced repeated failures to install a prepayment meter over seven months, missed appointments, broken promises, and threatening debt collection letters, compounded by poor handling of their priority service needs.

Please ensure this error of yours is rectified without delay!!
A/C #16164642361

author: John Brailsford
body:
When you receive an email advising you that your fixed rate period is ending soon and you need to act now, you usually go straight to the companies App or website.

Both were down for two days whilst being updated. This is very frustrating. The timing of the email should have been delayed until the updates were completed.

After eventually accessing your account and investigating the options, I chose one. There is then a 48 hour delay and no option to identify if you need to increase your DD.

I would have preferred the email to show best available option, which you can simply click on to view in detail and for it to show you any potential DD increase which you can then immediately implement.
insight: Poor timing of renewal notification coinciding with app downtime, combined with lack of transparency about Direct Debit changes and slow processing delays, created a frustrating customer experience.

It appears that Scottish power are happy for me and you to go through a long phone call process filtering calls before you can talk to a person that may not even be in this country to try and address any issues you have. author: Satish Pakki
body:
I honestly regret switching to the Scottish Power EV Optimise tariff. It *rarely* charges my Tesla Model 3 as promised, completely defeating the purpose of being on an EV-specific plan. The whole system feels unreliable and poorly thought out.

The app is especially awful — clunky, confusing, and most importantly, it just doesn’t work properly. I’ve lost count of how many times I’ve plugged in expecting a charge overnight, only to wake up and find nothing happened. It’s incredibly frustrating and makes owning an EV far more stressful than it should be.

I am switching back to Octopus as soon as possible — they actually understand how to deliver a smart charging service that works. Scottish Power really need to take notes because this is nowhere near good enough.

Avoid if you value reliability.
insight: The EV Optimise tariff and its associated app fail to reliably schedule overnight charging as promised, causing the service to be unreliable and frustrating for the customer.

author: Sarah
body:
AVOID Scottish Power AT ALL COSTS!!

They opened an account in my name without consent after I’d already closed and paid my balance after moving home, then added a missed payment to my credit file WITHOUT EVER CONTACTING ME TO DISCUSS WHY THERE WAS NO DIRECT DEBIT OR PAYMENTS BEING MADE FOR OVER 4 MONTHS. 

Their internal communication is non-existent, one team admits it’s an error while another continues to chase the debt and reject corrections through Experian. The people answering the phone don’t know their left from right - horrific experience and extremely concerning. 

I’ve sent evidence multiple times that I don’t lIve at the property they're trying to charge me for, been ignored, and had my complaint closed without resolution. Now escalating to the ombudsman 

Serious errors, zero accountability, and real impact on your credit. Do not risk it. Absolute morons.
insight: Account was opened without consent, missed payment added to credit file without contact, and multiple complaints were ignored with poor internal communication and lack of accountability.

insight: Customer is frustrated with slow refund processing, unresponsive support regarding an engineer visit, and perceived inconsistency in how quickly the utility processes charges versus credits.

body: Constantly increasing prices yet still providing the poor customer services. Waiting upto an hour in a queue then being cut off, eventually getting through to someone and being told we are unable to call you back from our call centres. Along wirh being given poor advice by your operators. 2/10 very poor customer service.
insight: Customer frustrated with rising prices, long call wait times, disconnections, and poor advice from support staff.

author: ADRIAN COOKE
body:
Appalling customer service.
Signed me up for an account by a third party estate agent with no proof of anything. 2 days before I signed the contract on the house. When I eventually managed to get hold of Scottish Power customer service to speak to someone , they need to know everything to pass a security check. Yet they had no information to check against before they can deal with a problem. 
Would not recommend to anyone. Not even my worst enemy….. Actually. They are my enemies.
insight: Customer frustrated by third-party account setup without proper verification and inability to resolve issues due to inadequate security check procedures.

author: .peter
body: problem sorted out
insight: User's problem was successfully resolved through customer service support.

author: Bryn Jones
body: Easy to use app to select new fixed tariff for the next twelve months
insight: User appreciated the straightforward process for selecting and switching to a new fixed tariff through the app.

author: MICHAEL
body: Smart meter not that good was told over a year ago meters had a know fault but never replaced
insight: Customer's faulty smart meter was not replaced despite reporting the known fault over a year ago, indicating poor service responsiveness.

author: Miss E Findlay
body: It was time to change my tariff and it was done very easily on the app. Scottish Power are very good at keeping me informed about my energy account and I love the “Opt ins” and the half price energy hours. Anytime I have had to contact the company, I have been met with courtesy and patience.
insight: User praises the app's ease of use for tariff changes, appreciates innovative energy-saving features, and commends the company's communication and customer service.

author: Rory
body:
Avoid at all costs! Customer service team have no idea! Issues registering on website so attempted via live chat and phone multiple times. Was told would be fixed as continued technical issues, nothing for 4 months despite me contacting them every fortnight. Eventually switched to a different company and they sent a completely inaccurate and made up bill. I went to ombudsman to dispute and finally came to a resolution and setup a payment plan. Then they sent a smaller and completely seperate bill to a debt collectors before sending me any warning! Worst company I have ever had to deal with. So so unhelpful! 



In response to the reply below, it has not yet been resolved and I am still waiting for a reply from your customer service team. I have contacted you several times and my name is Rory but thanks anyway!
insight: Customer experienced prolonged unresolved technical issues, inaccurate billing, and inadequate support responses across multiple contact channels, ultimately requiring ombudsman intervention.

author: Customer
body: Easy to continue with my current supplier.
insight: User appreciates the ease of managing their account and continuing their energy supply without friction.

author: Alec Berry
body: All was easy
insight: User had a straightforward and satisfactory experience with the app.

author: CHRISTINE BALLANTYNE
body: App doesn't work! Each time I use the app to enter meter readings it always says there is a problem and error then I have to phone readings which cost me money.
insight: Meter reading submission fails repeatedly in the app, forcing users to call and incur charges.

author: JOHN COLLINS
body:
I made a mistake yesterday.My App on Scottish Power was indicating that my current deal expires on 31 5.26 and recommended that I renew on a new contract to 31.5.27.
It transpired that I did NOT need to do this yesterday but after speaking to an Adviser named LISA I was assured that she would rectify my mistake and telephone me today  by 2 30 p.m.that she had done so.NO TELEPHONE CALL RECEIVED.According to my S.Power App the prices applicable on the new contract would apply right away ,a massive increase I would add. WHAT A WAY TO DO BUSINESS.
insight: Customer made an unwanted contract renewal based on app information, was promised a callback from an adviser to rectify the error, but the promised follow-up never occurred, leaving them locked into a higher-priced deal.

author: jackie laing
body: have not helped
insight: User feels the app or service has failed to resolve their issue or provide adequate support.

author: Mr Brian Mellor
body: Generally good service and good website. One exception though.  I requested a gas smartmeter 5 years ago and several times since. Still no date available.
insight: Customer has been waiting 5 years for a smart meter installation despite multiple requests with no resolution provided.

author: Mr Dinnen
body:
Not good I was in queue for callback but no one rang me. Your app put me through to house move  and not to tariff.

I gave up and went in to web site and made my tariff selection.
insight: User experienced callback queue failure and app routing to wrong department, requiring them to switch to the website to complete their tariff selection.

author: Steven Gilchrist
body: Every time I try and log into app it doesn't work it says my email address or password is wrong and I've got to go through the process of re registering my password it's so frustrating because in the past it was working great but now when I eventually get into the app it's completely different to before when I could check how much power I was using and check previous bills but none of that appears now so disappointing compared to how it was previously when I would give the app 5 star's but not now
insight: User experiences repeated authentication failures and missing functionality after app redesign, representing significant regression from previous version.

author: Hayley
body: Just the most horrific experience. It would take me hours to write out the experience I've had with Scottish Power, which is still ongoing. Just avoid unless you want to be left with money missing, going round in circles with their "complaints" team and constant lies. If your meter loses connection with them they will not help in any way.
insight: Customer experienced prolonged billing issues and poor support when their smart meter lost connection, with unresolved complaints and alleged dishonesty from the provider.

author: Robert A McLean
body: Easy to use and informative website
insight: User appreciates the website's ease of use and informative content.

author: Pamela Maybury
body: Changed tariff. No problem
insight: User successfully completed a tariff change with no issues encountered.

author: Nicola Bryne
body: I had to spend many hours on phone to change gas account to my name finaly had to raise a cmplaint to have this done also many emails back and forth for them to ackowlege payments made on account  late payment letters sent to myself causing stres thankfully pspoke to a lovely customer service lady who sorted it all for me then and there.
insight: Customer experienced significant delays and errors in account transfer and payment processing, requiring complaints and multiple contacts to resolve, though ultimately satisfied with individual staff member's support.

author: Sue F
body: Scottish Power have loads of price deals when you try and select the one is best for you they say you cannot have it only one or two that they choose for you. Total deception and should not be allowed. If you pay your bills you lose out with Scottish Power. Went to a better provider with a lot lower tariff and fees.
insight: Customer feels misled by restricted tariff options and switched to a competitor offering better rates and lower fees.

author: Carlton Cruise
body: We left them in January this year to go to octopus energy our bills where averaging over 200 a month  when come to submit final readings  ourselves had gone back to zero  I have asked for paper rolls to be sent to us still nothing
insight: Customer switched providers due to high bills and experienced issues with final meter readings resetting to zero plus unresolved requests for paper billing materials.

author: andrew
body: 24 hours ago I phoned to ask advice re our tariff that expires soon. Ask for a call back as you suggested rather than waiting in a long queue. Needless to say no one has bothered to respond to my call. Maybe we should consider another supplier with a better customer service ….
insight: Customer's callback request was not fulfilled, leading to frustration and consideration of switching suppliers.

author: Andy Cooper
body:
Easy to update to new rate and clear info
one major complaint!
unable to get a smart meter supplied and fitted, been inquiring for years
insight: User praises the app's functionality but is frustrated by years of inability to obtain and install a smart meter.

author: louise  o
body:
Scottish power agreed a tariff but have been charging me a more expensive tariff for a year. 
When I queried it was spoken to in a patronising manor as if I was at fault. 
Have had to spend hours on the phone trying to sort it, still not sorted! 
Have been charged late payment charges even though my account is in credit. Followed be threatening letters.
insight: Customer was incorrectly charged a higher tariff for a year, treated dismissively when complaining, and received late payment charges despite being in credit, with the issue remaining unresolved after extensive contact attempts.

author: Jean Glynn
body: Easy to change tariff
insight: User appreciates the simplicity of changing their energy tariff through the app.

author: Richard Lynch
body: Quite straightforward to change tariff,but the same old gripe of cost of electricity (and gas) at moment,it's getting out of control again for everyone
insight: User acknowledges app functionality works well but expresses concern about rising energy prices beyond the company's control.

author: Stuart Mcquade
body: I tried telephoning and using the online chat to get information on EV tarrifs with no success. Due to the war in Iran I was under pressure to fix so I did renew with Scottish Power but I will change supplier after this year is complete as other companies are offering better rates. Disapointed that I could not get through to talk to someone.
insight: Customer unable to reach support via phone or chat regarding EV tariffs and switched suppliers due to poor service and uncompetitive pricing.

author: Cecilia
body: I hate profiteering as this (one of the “big six” energy companies the UK) does. My bill went UP x3 after a meter reading! I only done a meter reading 3 weeks prior…. from owing £40 went to £153!!!!!!!, and trying to get anywhere with CS team is another headache. Of course cannot change companies right now as that is an EXTRA CHARGE TO LEAVE!!! DISGUSTING
insight: Customer experienced a sudden tripling of their bill following a meter reading and found the customer service team unhelpful in resolving the issue, compounded by exit fees preventing them from switching providers.

author: Mr David Martin
body: I've been with Scottish Power for a number of years and am pleased with their service provided and the easy usage of their related app.
insight: Long-term customer satisfied with Scottish Power's service quality and app usability despite giving 4 out of 5 stars.

author: Mr Kitchen
body:
The lady I spoke to was very helpful and sorted my problem out swiftly.
On the down side there was a huge amount of background noise which made it very difficult to hear her.
insight: Staff member was helpful and resolved the issue quickly, but call quality was poor due to excessive background noise.

author: Richard
body:
Reasonably easy to navigate.
Few options offered (2).
Unable to find standard variable tariff details.
No follow up email to confirm my change.
Cooling off period appeared to be for new customers only.
insight: User found the app navigation adequate but encountered significant limitations with tariff options, lack of confirmation communication, and unclear cooling-off period terms.

author: Tony Mahoney
body:
Setting half price periods was a nightmare 
I missed out on the April reset
insight: User found the interface for configuring off-peak periods confusing and missed a seasonal billing reset as a result.

author: Rachel
body: I wanted to keep my d/d the same as previous amount bearing in mind if I owe any money at the end of the month I clear the debt, I tried to change the d/d but was unable to as Scottish Power wanted me to pay £360.00 on my card??? Went on the chat and spoke to Arup but he was unable to do anything as he needed a gas reading?? What is the problem I am never in debt this has put me off Scottish Power I will be changing at the end if my contract…
insight: Customer was unable to update their direct debit amount through the app and received an unexpected payment demand, with chat support unable to resolve the issue due to missing information.

author: James Wherry
body: You have asked me to comment before your advisor has completed her work. I'm waiting to hear from her on Tuesday...
insight: User is waiting for their advisor to complete work and feels the review request came prematurely.

author: Wayne Robert Carter
body:
Managed to sort out credit onto meter
Good communication and everything worked as it should with text message
insight: User successfully added credit to their meter and appreciated the clear communication via text notifications.

author: Nicholas Dowle
body: Just upgraded tariff,I found it easy to do and was quick and efficient.
insight: User had a seamless and quick experience upgrading their energy tariff through the app.

author: Naomi Kerans
body: All was good  & Hugo was very helpful BUT it takes a very long time  to actually SPEAK to someone… surely this could be made easier for the elderly & others on your list …
insight: While staff assistance was helpful, the lengthy wait time to reach a representative is a significant barrier for elderly and vulnerable customers.

author: Robert Ruoff
body: excellent service after initial problems on signing up
insight: User experienced initial signup difficulties but was satisfied with the resolution and overall service quality.

author: Adrian Palmer
body: Scottish Power supply me with electricity and have done for 11 years. The power arrives at my flat no problem. But they are a nightmare to deal with. The website is so difficult to use, the automatic meter reading works only intermittently and the charges and tariffs are always deliberately difficult to understand.  I have dealt with other suppliers at other properties and they are all the same.
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with Scottish Power's difficult website interface, unreliable automatic meter readings, and opaque charging structures despite 11 years of reliable power supply.

author: Ian Clarke
body: After being repeatedly contacted for the best part of a year to have a smart meter installed and making the time to have said meter installed, I find it astonishing that you then do not have the technology in place to actually use it in the manner intended and that I still have to take the readings for you.
insight: Customer is frustrated that the utility company cannot read the smart meter remotely despite aggressive push for installation, requiring manual readings to continue.

author: Carole
body: it takes to long to get in touch but service is good
insight: Customer is satisfied with service quality but frustrated by slow response times when contacting support.

author: Miss Maguire
body: The person on the line dealt with what I wanted him to do. He explained everything.
insight: Customer service representative provided clear explanations and resolved the user's issue satisfactorily.

author: Mr William Mcleary
body: Customer services were of no help at all and insisted on giving me contradictory information and information that was plain wrong. I am in the process of moving to another provider, that's how poor a service I  am receiving from Scottish Power.
insight: Customer received contradictory and incorrect information from customer service, leading to dissatisfaction and plan to switch providers.

author: David Armstrong
body:
Website worked well once you got used to it. 
Not easy like many companies to speak to a human.
insight: Website functionality is adequate once learned, but customer service accessibility via phone support is difficult.

author: Miss L B
body:
I can’t wait to switch! I have had an open complaint with Scottish Power since the 5th December! 4 months! They tried tricking me into going onto a pre payment meter! Wasn’t in debt and paid monthly! They have scheduled call backs countless times, no call! Email with replies as that are completely irrelevant! They change the “status” of the complaint constantly! (Offer with, “waiting for more information from customer”) which is completely untrue! Bullies is the best way to describe this company!
To add, never EVER provided me with a smart meter appliance so that I can view my usage (been a customer years)
Oh, and watch yourselves! If you stop paying direct debit (even when not in contract) and just pay your bill every month your fees go up (and I mean sky rocket)!
Compare your prices but if you want a complete with any levels of customer service…… use someone else!!!!
insight: Customer experienced prolonged complaint mishandling, aggressive debt collection tactics, lack of smart meter provision, and unexpected fee increases for non-direct debit payments.

author: Nicola Webber
body: I have been charged for 1 day around 10 days prior to switching. I wasn’t getting readings when trying to access my account. Found out after 45 minutes chatting to your customer support team who should have noticed this within 5 minutes. Changed to half hour readings. Couldn’t access app or account for a while. No option showing to change meter to half hour readings.
insight: Customer experienced multiple service failures including incorrect billing, account access issues, and slow customer support response to a meter reading problem.

author: Val Waterman
body: Helpful staff just a bit annoyed that im not able to get an indoor meter
insight: Customer appreciated helpful staff but is dissatisfied with being unable to obtain an indoor meter installation.

author: Claire Brown
body: Woman was quick but phone line was very crackly hard to hear
insight: Customer service representative was responsive but call quality was poor due to audio interference.

author: Mr Marriott
body: Seemed straight forward to change contract but haven’t received confirmation of it.
insight: User successfully initiated a contract change but did not receive confirmation, creating uncertainty about the transaction status.

author: Fiona
body: The lady that I spoke to on the phone was very helpful and professional. Hopefully all has been resolved regarding starting our new fixed term contract with Scottish power. Thank you.
insight: Customer praised phone support for helpfulness and professionalism in setting up a new fixed-term contract.

author: Lolly
body: Scottish power have the worst customer service ever! Rude, arrogant and not interested in helping you in anyway, shape or form, they increase prices every 3 months and don't think to inform you! AVOID, AVOID!
insight: Customer experienced poor customer service interactions and complained about frequent price increases without adequate notification.

author: Mr Sunah Malik  Dilip Pashikan
body: Excellent customer service
insight: User praised the quality of customer service provided by the utility.

author: A Byford
body: I have used ScottishPower for many years now and they tell me some are cheaper, but I’ve always been pleased with the price they’re given me and I think overall I’ve always got a better and cheaper deal
insight: Customer is satisfied with ScottishPower's pricing and feels they receive competitive deals compared to other providers.

author: Janice West
body: It was a good response and my query was dealt with.
insight: User was satisfied with the speed and effectiveness of customer service response to their query.

author: Stephen Graham
body: Changing Tariff was easy to do. . .but still went up in price.
insight: User found the tariff change process straightforward but was disappointed that the new tariff resulted in higher prices.

author: Gary Seccombe
body: Waiting time was acceptable. Assistant was knowledgeable and very helpful.
insight: Customer had a positive experience with responsive and knowledgeable support staff.

author: ANN UNWIN
body: Ive been with scottish power for many years . And they've been useless . I report smart meter not working nothing was done gas meter not working so you can get reading from it, they sent 2 people out who was not told that they needed to replace meter. Nothing was done , then im in credit then im in 600 plus dept even tho I pay my bills every month off 300 pounds ,
insight: Customer experiences unresolved smart meter and gas meter issues compounded by unexplained billing discrepancies despite regular payments.

author: Joy
body: It was not easy to find where to change personal details. Could be much simpler
insight: User found the process for updating personal details difficult to navigate and unintuitive.

author: MRGRAHAMSMITH
body: I found the video on the text message not helpful at all. It didnt explain things on how to read meters with duel readings I had to go onto YOU TUBE on how to read the meter. I find the text message reminder helpful
insight: The in-app video guidance for reading dual meters was unclear, forcing the user to seek help elsewhere on YouTube, though text message reminders were appreciated.

author: MR R WALTERS
body:
I like that part of my payments to to a charity . 
I don't like the fact that my gas smart gas meter has never worked from the day it was put in , and no effort to fix it has been made also , I never ever received an in house reader since installation .
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with a non-functional gas smart meter and lack of follow-up support despite repeated issues since installation.

author: G Wyles
body: used to be ok. now have to keep on verifying password. running in chrome constantly. updated version still doesn't resolve problem. Hopeless. Recent updates make no difference. They have ruined the app.
insight: Repeated password verification issues persist despite multiple app updates, causing user frustration.

author: Customer
body:
Had on going issues with both my Gas & Electric for 5 years popped from pillar to post ,was a very stressful time not going into it all as SP Resolved all issues many thanks to a lovely lady who worked extremely hard, kept me up to date with everything that was going on. I can now breathe a little better & sleep well too.
A MASSIVE Thankyou to Veronika at SP RESOLUTION DEPARTMENT for everything she done for me Veronika is a outstanding asset for SP...Thankyou soo
insight: Customer had long-standing issues with gas and electric accounts that were resolved by an excellent customer service representative named Veronika, resulting in significant relief and satisfaction.

author: Satisfied Dad
body: Dreadful company. Impossible to speak to a human being. Steer clear of them at all costs. Had solar installed and had to fight to get them to pay the SEG. I would give a minus rating if I could. Do not use them: better to pay more to a company that cares about its customers!!! Be warned they are shockingly awful.
insight: Customer experienced severe difficulties contacting the company and had to fight to receive Solar Export Guarantee payments.

author: Marion
body: Too soon to say. I want to wait to ensure the change in tariff goes smoothly
insight: User is withholding judgment until their tariff change processes successfully through the app.

author: ROBERT SMITH
body: Dealt with online.
insight: User appreciated being able to resolve their issue through the online platform.

author: Tom Kernan
body: A very professional company. Geared up to work online, so i never have to call Scottish Power's traditional customer service, saving time and effort. Some companies make a big pitch about the quality of their customer service. whereas I find I get better and faster service, using Scottish Power Online
insight: Customer appreciates the efficiency and convenience of Scottish Power's online service compared to traditional phone-based customer support.

author: Customer S
body: Had a member of Scottish power complaints team call me back regards to the missing money when switching from prepaid to direct debt. complete and utter waste of time didn’t have a answer to why or how, kept changing her mind, stuttering and contradicting herself.
insight: Customer experienced poor support from complaints team regarding missing funds during prepaid to direct debit transfer, with staff providing inconsistent and contradictory explanations.

author: Mavis Yates
body: I have no problem except why do I have to read my meter when I have a smart meter. I am almost 84 years of age and it is almost impossible for me to read as it is under the stairs and at floor level .what is the point of having a smart meter !!!
insight: Elderly customer frustrated that they must manually read their smart meter despite its installation, citing physical accessibility difficulties.

author: rob gladwin
body: Changed my tariff and a week later still being charged on the old one also requested a smart display unit and got no response.  Super poor service.
insight: Customer experienced delayed tariff updates causing incorrect billing and received no response to a smart display unit request.

author: Alastair
body:
It's been ok, one or two blips, with smart meters, but I've come out of it feeling ok. Still don't know how much gas I'm using, because the remote display in my kitchen only shows electric. I've told customer service but nothing happened. 
I've been trying to save energy by doing my laundry at the weekends between 11 and 4  both Saturday and Sunday. I've been asked to choose weekday hours in addition, but have stuck to the weekends. Unfortunately, insread of having 10 half price hours. I now only have 8 hours. Not sure how much of a benefit this is to me (sic)  
I've had to choose an other fixed tariff as mine is ending.  Fairly painless process, so far.
insight: Customer experiences unresolved smart meter display issues and confusion about time-of-use tariff benefits, though overall process satisfaction is moderate.

author: Mr Ashworth
body: Poor layout to allow decision making a tariff chang
insight: The app's interface makes it difficult for users to compare and select different energy tariffs.

author: Nicole Ford
body: They are terrible electricity business ever chase you up for debt thats not yours dont see why they havent been shut down yet they harrass people everyday and its beyond a joke wouldnt reccomend them to anyone
insight: Customer reports aggressive debt collection practices for charges they dispute and poor treatment by the company.

author: Valerie DALY
body: I found it difficult to get through to someone to speak to as I don't think it is easy for older people. However my friend managed to get through and the woman I spoke to was really helpful.
insight: Customer service quality is good, but accessibility barriers exist for older users trying to reach support channels.

author: Christine Healey
body: Always reads my smart meter on time and forward my bill via email. Always keeps me informed if I’m paying enough or reduce payments if I’m overpaying. As well as half price electricity on days to suit you 8 hours a week.
insight: User appreciates reliable smart meter readings, accurate billing communications, and flexible payment options including time-of-use electricity discounts.

author: Graham Key
body: You just make everything easy to find what is right for me 👍
insight: User appreciates the app's intuitive navigation and ability to quickly find relevant information.

author: nick ellaby
body:
Efficient and explanatory once call was accepted
Realised easier to deal with the call pm as pre 12.00pm lines v busy
Happy to tie in to new tariff until May 2027
insight: Customer appreciated efficient and clear support once connected, noting that calling after noon reduces wait times and successfully agreed to a new tariff extension.

author: George Smith
body: Very helpful staff
insight: User praises the helpfulness of the customer service staff.

author: Mr Wilson
body: Not getting through on calls
insight: User is frustrated with difficulty reaching customer support by phone.

author: Keith Casey
body: Everything was easy to do
insight: User found the app interface intuitive and straightforward to navigate.

author: kathleen
body: Advisor was helpful and listened to my enquiry
insight: Customer praised the advisor for being attentive and responsive to their enquiry.

author: Kathleen Pringle
body: Everything I do is through my phone on line and never have any bother at all. I n mine contact
insight: User is satisfied with the online app experience and finds it easy to manage their account through their phone.

author: GEORGE BATCHELOR
body: Very easy and straightforward
insight: User appreciates the app's intuitive design and ease of use.

author: Karen
body: Terrible customer service
insight: User is dissatisfied with the quality of customer service provided by the utility.

author: Garth Thomas
body: A truly dreadful company...impossible to communicate with. I have moved to a more expensive electricity supplier just so I don't have to deal with Scottish Power ever again.
insight: Customer switched to a more expensive supplier due to poor communication and unresolved issues with Scottish Power.

author: Wendy
body: loving the app easier then walking a 20 mins walk with my arthritis in my ankles and knees. thank you
insight: User appreciates the app for providing convenient access to their account without requiring physical travel, which is particularly beneficial for those with mobility issues.

author: C Robinson
body: Lack of many different contracts   no free standing charge contracts   not much choice
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with the limited range of contract options available, particularly the absence of free standing charge contracts.

author: Guest
body: Keeps you informed,app is easy to use.
insight: User appreciates the app's ease of use and ability to keep them informed about their energy account.

author: No
body: The advice given was clear and helpful to enable me to choose a new account. My questions were answered well.
insight: Customer received clear, helpful guidance and responsive support when selecting a new energy account.

author: Anne L
body: I am trying to find out why I am being charged £10 a week “debt collection fee” when I have a prepay meter that has always been in credit. The responses are all AI generated so I am just going round in circles. Terrible service!
insight: Customer is being charged unexplained debt collection fees on a prepay account in credit and receiving only automated responses that fail to resolve the issue.

author: MARGARET WILLIAMS
body: Agent helpful and knowledgeable but I found it difficult to hear because of the background noise in the call centre. I was constantly hearing other agents’ conversations
insight: Agent was competent but the call centre environment had excessive background noise that impaired communication quality.

author: Gtoc
body: No problems
insight: User is satisfied with the app's overall functionality and performance.

author: Theo
body: The advisor was polite, well-informed and gave me accurate information but the news that my smart meter will still not work for some time makes me think that Scottish Power should be putting more pressure on the company providing the services enabling meters to communicate. That’s why I call my meter a dumb meter. It will only be smart when it works.
insight: Customer praises the advisor's professionalism but is deeply frustrated that Scottish Power's smart meter remains non-functional due to inadequate pressure on service providers.

author: Nigel Howlett
body: I was asked to send information to someone called Cory, how  do I it PLEASE😡😡😡😡😡😡😡
insight: User is frustrated with unclear instructions on how to contact or send information to a customer service representative named Cory.

author: James Jaggard
body: Good service
insight: User is satisfied with the overall service quality provided by the utility.

author: Lynda
body: She was nice polite and helped with what I asked her name I think was miranda xxxx
insight: User had a positive interaction with a helpful and polite customer service representative named Miranda.

author: David Bartle
body: very good. No further comment required.
insight: User is satisfied with the app's overall performance and functionality.

author: Sheelagh
body: I have had help understanding  how the bill works thank you
insight: Customer appreciated assistance with understanding their energy bill despite giving a 4-star rather than 5-star rating.

author: Mr Alan  Haresnape
body: Prompt decisive decisions made over phone
insight: User appreciates the company's quick and decisive handling of their issue via phone support.

author: Mr DAVID OWEN
body: It was a simple enough process but your website is always annoying, for example: (1) it says I haven’t submitted a meter reading for some time - I did so a month ago and you billed me accordingly (2) as to the new tariff - you offered me one price and said it had reduced to another figure why not give me the best figure at the start
insight: User experiences inaccurate meter reading records and unclear tariff pricing presentation despite completing the process successfully.

author: Mrs Jones
body: Provided me with the fixed rate details very helpful
insight: User found the app helpful for accessing and understanding their fixed rate tariff details.

author: Shiela Ewing
body: Efficient but brusque
insight: The app functions well and meets its purpose effectively, though the user experience could benefit from a more friendly and approachable interface.

author: Irene Finnie
body: The assistant was very helpful and I was pleased with the help I received.  However I was dealing with a lady South Africa and must admit I could not always understand what she was saying!!   Why can Scottish powers call centre not be based in SCOTLAND!!!
insight: Customer appreciated the assistance received but expressed frustration about the call centre being located outside Scotland with communication difficulties due to the agent's accent.

author: MRSJANETTURNER
body: the gentleman was very nice but did keep waffling on about things i didnt need to know.
insight: Customer appreciated the staff member's politeness but felt the interaction included unnecessary information that reduced overall satisfaction.

author: CAROL
body: Took forever to get through on phone. Very helpful Customer Service. Only drawback, was background noise, so many calls going on, so many voices in background, I could not hear, kept asking lady to repeat what she said. Call took so much longer for that reason.
insight: Customer Service staff were helpful but call duration was significantly extended due to poor call centre acoustics and background noise interference.

author: Alexander Brown
body: Polite, respectful and very helpful.
insight: User praised the politeness, respect, and helpfulness of customer service staff.

author: Carol Aikin
body: Easy to follow in app, never had any issues, smart meter fitted.
insight: User appreciates the intuitive app interface and successful smart meter installation despite giving 4 out of 5 stars.

author: BRYAN
body: Easy to change my tariff on the App. I was able to compare the different tariffs available choose which option suited me and set it up.
insight: User successfully compared and switched energy tariffs through the app with ease.

Total Gas and Electricity cost was £91.81
VAT was £4.60 
So my end balance should be £236.97 but £4.75 has been deducted without any explanations and end balance shown was £232.22
insight: Customer is charged an unexplained £4.75 fee that doesn't match their bill calculations.

author: Linda Price

The staff I dealt with were helpful understanding and seemed knowledgeable.

When I phoned the assistant was just going to read out the tariffs available! But in order to make a choice you need to see all the information including the TERMS and CONDITIONS. I don't think he appreciated the problem. He advised me just to log on to the SP website and do it my self without following the links in the email! Also, he wanted to know if I owned my house! Why is this information important in buying energy! VERY UNHAPPY EXPERIENCE.
insight: Customer frustrated by broken email links, unhelpful phone support, and unclear data collection practices when trying to switch energy tariffs.

Good friendy help when swapping over,
Kept apraised of what was happening through out.

I had sent a message asking for someone to come out to do a meter reading as I have no idea how to take one as there is a request for me to send a reading other than that I have nothing to complain about, just love being back with Scottish power 
              Kind regards D.Hickmott
insight: Customer is satisfied with the service overall but needs assistance with meter reading functionality, requesting a home visit from a technician.

author: John Donnelly

author: STEPHEN JEFFERY
body:
We have waited 11 Months to have our meter moved, Yesterday was the arranged day for works to be carried out,
SSEN, and my Electrian did their works perfectly.
After waiting for sometime for the Meter company to arrive I decided to phone yourselves just to make sure  they were on route only to be informed that they had no record of the job to be done...
Your representative was a credit to your company in arranging an emergency meter company to attend, they did and were very good.
I would like an explanation as to how this could have happened, thank goodness I called.on the off chance...
Yours,
Mr Jeffery.
PS. The Job was arranged for yesterday 13th April.
insight: Critical service failure where meter installation job was not logged in the system, requiring customer intervention to prevent a missed appointment despite 11 months of waiting.

author: Neil Lomax

This is not helped by the fact that I an 77 years old and can't see the meter I an supposed to photograph.  I have given up l hope of ever getting any payment for spare electric I am producing!
insight: The solar panel buyback form is unnecessarily complex and inaccessible, causing repeated form submission failures and preventing elderly users from claiming payment for exported electricity.

Was told the technology was not in place to remove me from the old category (double end pan) which I found quite strange. Hopefully they will sort it out as I only have 1 pension to live on at the moment.

author: Mr Karl Bohn

Strangely enough I could not find my review!! 
Odd, because it was all true? 
I would go as far to say there is a whiff of conspiracy with a huge lack of transparency.

insight: The app's frequent forced logouts and cumbersome re-authentication process frustrate users, particularly when biometric security options like Face ID could provide better security without sacrificing usability.

Thank you Scottish power .
insight: Long-term customer expresses satisfaction with reliable service and appreciates the company's commitment to supporting rural communities despite higher infrastructure costs.

body:
I was with Octopus for a few years and contacting them was very difficult and slow IMO. 
Scottish Power had good reviews and so far after a shaky start they are real improvement to Octopus 
Charging control is easy by comparison and tech support quick and helpful. 
Long may it continue
insight: User switched from Octopus to Scottish Power and reports significantly improved customer service responsiveness and technical support quality.

Scott the complaints handler is full of false promises that he never seems to fulfill emailed him daily and not had a reply in nearly two weeks absolute joke of a company. This now leaves me back at square one and today I’ve started the small claims proceeding against Scottish power as you didn’t want to help nor work with me. I really hope no one else has to get put through this mental health strain that this company has caused me over the last year.

Takia also emailed me some information and numbers for organisations that I can get in touch with about my other debts.

It was good to speak to a person and the person I spoke to was understanding and respectful.
I do not find Scottish Power's policy of adding £15 for late payments helpful. The bill payer is disabled and only gets to the bank at the end of the month. So she accrues £15 per month which makes her bill unreal for her. Unfortunately she does not do Direct Debits.
I will try and find a way to sort this out.
Meanwhile your representative was very helpful in the circumstances.
insight: Customer with disability faces financial hardship due to £15 monthly late payment fees, though staff provided supportive assistance.

author: Rachel Peat
body:
Chris on the phone was very helpful
BUT I  had to call as the previous week an engineer came to fit a new smart meter - one that might connect with SP.  The third time this would have been changed - it has NEVER connected.  He told me it was not the meter but the hub needing changed and as it was not on his job sheet he couldn't do it.
  Despite having a van full of hubs.
So I have to arrange yet another appointment.
Why was I not told this by the 2 previous engineers?  Or in previous phone conversations with your call centre - when I havd explained the problem in detail?
insight: Smart meter installation has failed three times and the root cause (faulty hub) was not identified by previous engineers or call centre staff, requiring multiple appointment reschedules.

author: Sarah Thomas
body: I have been with Scottish Power for almost a year, I moved from Boost. SP app is good its functions allows me to manage and monitor my gas usage, there was an instance where it glitched whilst I made a payment which caused a problem. An error code came up so i tried again and again, when i checked the bank there was three pending transactions. I had to wait 5 days to see if SP took the money ask them to reimburse me the money if they didnt take it it bounce back to my account. SP were obviously aware of this but continued to ring throughout the 5 days.  Everytime inspoke to someone knew and said they hadn't received the payment and didnt know why they hadn't received it even though i told I had paid it and it came out three times and it stuck in pending. They kept saying there was no record of my calls. After 5 days the money bounced back and I made the payment. I found the whole experience really stressful
insight: A payment app glitch created duplicate transactions that caused significant stress and poor customer service handling during the resolution process.

author: Monty

I would leave no stars if possible.

I was apparently being over charged by my previous supplier they told me and said they would get the money back and put my account in credit. Only to be told I have to deal with it my self so obviously the sales girl was lying. I was then told my usage would be between 67-75 pound for my property and the 1st direct debit was for 93.99🤣 they took this money from my bank and I haven't even been with them 1 week. I would not recommend to anyone lucky they don't charge you to leave cause I'm going else where.
insight: Customer feels deceived by sales staff regarding credit promises and receives a direct debit significantly higher than quoted estimate within the first week.

author: Anuradha Deb
body:
Incompetent and most unprofessional energy provider I have ever come across. I closed my account and moved out of my flat recently giving them my last energy readings and requesting my final bill and a refund for any credit left over. On the phone I had spoken to an advisor and gave her the readings and my correspondence address and was told everything was up to date. Three weeks later, I logged into the account and I just realise I have a lot of outstanding credit built up that’s owed to me on the account, so I contacted them on the chat to find out how to have this refunded and to ensure my account was finalised and closed. 2 hours on the chat, delayed responses, no communication telling me what they were doing, an advisor called aieshwariya was who I was speaking to (and my god was the whole conversation the most frustrating thing I have ever experienced), took 1 hour to look into the account and barely replyed. I wasted another 2 hours having already wasted 2 weeks when I thought they had already finalised  everything there end. 

I’ve now been told there is a glitch in the system so they can’t issue a final bill. Apparently, the meter readings I gave 2 weeks ago was inputted incorrectly by the advisor I spoke to on the phone, and  she also messed up my correspondence address which was spelt incorrectly!! 

Meaning I have to now wait a minimum of another 24-48 hours (supposedly) for the bill to be generated only because I insisted as they said it could take another 7 days due to their system glitch, and the previous advisor’s incompetence. To top it off, I’ve always paid by direct debit for several years but despite this, I’ve been told  the credit owed to me would need to be refunded via cheque despite paying by direct debit and sent to my correspondence address- due to delays caused by them, this could take up to 20 days to be issued. 

The whole process has been a joke! Complete incompetence, poorly trained advisors and a complete lack of urgency. 

They will delay paying you what’s owed and yet hound you if your even a few hours over payment gate, then sell your data to scammers when you close an account so scammers harass you with calls 3-4 x a day for over a week either selling you their services or demanding a payment that doesn’t exist! Which has happened to me and the calls are relentless! 

DO NOT USE! Awful company! Use octopus, their customer service is unmatched!
insight: Customer experienced significant delays and errors in account closure process, including incorrect meter readings and address entry by advisors, combined with poor chat support and unfavorable refund procedures.

author: Kevin Herbert
body: I rang Scottish Power yesterday to complain about the huge increase in monthly payment for electricity that they had decided was right for me. It was absolute nonsense so I spoke to a very helpful employee who eventually agreed with me that the figure I had calculated was more realistic. For the last 2 years it has been a nightmare dealing with Scottish Power. A new so called smart meter was installed so they could have an auto reading  - it has never worked properly and it was installed so low to the floor I cannot read it so they have to send someone to the house occasionally to read the meter. My advice to everybody is avoid Scottish Power!
insight: Customer experienced long-standing issues with malfunctioning smart meter and inflated billing, though customer service eventually provided support.

author: Sange
body: Scottish Power were the supplier in a property i moved into in December 2025, i changed supplier immediately to EON. I used gas and electricity for a day or two in the process of changing. I paid the bill in full. Then this week April 2026, i noticed they had put a file on my credit report saying i owed then £1.53, can you believe it? No communication, no letter or contact, as far as i was concerned the bill had been paid in full. I dont even know what the £1.53 was for. I paid £5 to the account number, still ongoing. I phoned them and the person on the phone said the £5  had gone to the gas and the £1.53 was for electricity, are they that desperate to hurt people over that amount. Anyway they couldnt transfer from gas to electric in their OWN SYSTEM? So, over the phone i had to pay £1.53 and now wait 10 days for my £5 back. Crazy company, who i will NEVER, do business with again. No wonder they lose so many customer and have a bad reputation for not dealing with customers and complaints effectively. Shameful to affect someones credit rating for £1.53.  Unhappy customer indeed.
insight: Customer's credit file was negatively impacted by a £1.53 outstanding balance with no prior communication, and the company's system failed to properly allocate a payment between gas and electricity accounts.

author: James
body:
On April 1st I entered my G&E readings in the wrong boxes on their site. It wasn't until the 3rd that I realised my (costly) mistake and so I wrote to them, via email. I was advised to wait 48 hours "perhaps a little longer" and sure enough, on the morning of the 6th, they replied asking for photos of my meters to confirm the actual readings.

I replied approximately 30 minutes later and within the hour I received a phone call (Easter Monday no less) where the issue was discussed, my mind put at ease and the mistake rectified.

An hour later I received an email confirming my updated readings and that everything was now back to normal. An online check also confirmed this.

Well done SP - a first class customer service and a problem dealt with in just a few days. You've earned this 5 star review.
insight: Customer made a billing error but praised the company's responsive and helpful customer service team for quickly resolving the issue despite the Easter Monday holiday.

I was told that I should have realised as I was asked to submit meter readings after 3 months. I don’t have a smart gas meter so this didn’t seem unreasonable to me, until I questioned it on 2nd bill 
Rubbish service and I will leave as soon as exit fee period is over would not recommend, enticed me to them with an offer of saving money which has never happened, regrets signing with them
insight: Customer's weekend electricity discount was never applied due to smart meter reading failures, and poor communication about the issue has led to loss of trust and intent to switch providers.

author: Victoria
body:
I have been with Scottish Power for over a decade and I can't fault them. Their staff in their call centres are very friendly and very knowledgeable. They don't push products onto you - if you say no they accept it. 
But usually I can do everything I need online. Their app is great and allows you to manage your account, sign up for new things like the power saver and rewards and control your direct debit. It shows you your usage and I love that you can compare it to previous years. If you do need to speak to someone there is an online chat feature or the phone number. 
I love the power saver initiative where I can select 8 hours a week when I receive half price electricity.
insight: Long-term customer praises ScottishPower's friendly staff, intuitive app functionality, and innovative power-saving tariff options.
