insight: User praises app usability and emergency response reliability, though questions the efficiency of resource allocation during leak repair.

author: Alun T
body: What are they doing? Have to delete and reinstall the app every time I try to log in now. Absolutely ridiculous.
insight: User experiences repeated login failures requiring app reinstallation, indicating a critical authentication or stability issue.

author: Phillip
body: Very helpful, polite and friendly.
insight: User praised the politeness, friendliness, and helpfulness of customer service staff.

author: Aileen McLaughlin
body:
Good information 
Well presented
insight: User appreciates the app's clear information presentation and layout design.

author: Janette Johnston
body: By the way I’ve been treated.
insight: User is satisfied with how they have been treated by the company.

author: Daphne Startup
body: not managed to get to enter my meter readings yet
insight: User is unable to submit meter readings through the app, indicating a functional or usability problem with this core feature.

author: john todd
body: apart from repeatidly trying to get me to fit a smart meter., latest is that my meter has reached its end of life, and needs changing before it gives false readings, i have agreed to have a 2nd meter fitted, but I DO NOT WANT A SMART METER, scot power say they cant fit one, my local mp says they can, i,m at end of life stage, 82yrs young, too much interference already
insight: Customer is frustrated by repeated pressure to install a smart meter despite explicitly declining it, and feels caught between conflicting advice from ScottishPower and their MP.

author: D.R EDWARDS
body: I would not be any other way other than truthful...always very helpful.
insight: User praises the company's honesty and helpfulness in customer interactions.

author: Glyn
body: Happy with the offer another fixed rate deal.
insight: Customer is satisfied with securing another fixed rate energy deal through the app.

author: Catherine Anne Docherty
body: Very helpful when you phone customer service
insight: User had a positive experience with ScottishPower's phone customer service team.

author: tiby katy
body: Havent been able to log in for over a week. They will not give a time frame of when it will be fixed. Worked fine before the update will be changing suppliers would not reccomend.
insight: User unable to log in for over a week following an app update with no communication on resolution timeline, leading to churn risk.

author: Ellis Hedley
body: I want to pay what I chose to pay not what you tel me to, I’m not happy about it!
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with fixed billing amounts and wants control over their payment choices.

author: Gsry
body: Very informative service
insight: User appreciates the informative nature of the service provided by the app.

author: Judith Hunt
body: Keeping us updated well and offered help
insight: Customer appreciates proactive communication and helpful support from ScottishPower.

author: frank welpa
body: Took repeated phone calls to resolve a straightforward issue, they failed to provide the explanation I requested, wasted literally HOURS of my time. Appalling!
insight: Customer experienced poor customer service with multiple phone calls required to resolve a simple issue and lack of adequate explanation.

author: Nick Barker
body: I’m happy with my supply, disappointed it costs me more because I’m not on a DD. I never will be with any supplier to stop suggesting it to me.
insight: Customer is unhappy about paying a premium for not using direct debit and frustrated by repeated suggestions to switch to this payment method.

author: J S
body: best
insight: User expresses strong satisfaction with the app or service.

author: Steffan Harris
body: I’ll give 4 stars! But  as I moved to Scottish power from a previous provider and still have there smart meter display. Which does not pick up the gas meter any more and I have to manual read the gas meter .. I have been told they can’t send me a new devise .. I’ve had spinal surgery and have rods in my back .. having to get on the floor in the cupboard to read the Meter causes me a lot of pain and discomfort..
insight: User cannot obtain a replacement smart meter display that reads gas, forcing manual meter readings that cause physical pain due to a medical condition.

author: Norman Harvey
body: Over the last 12 months I have been very happy with Scottish Power. The smart metre works well in conjunction with the Scottish Power App. All easily accessible and checking daily consumption + changing Direct Debit amounts and requesting refunds All works really well and hope it continues.
insight: User is satisfied with the smart meter integration, app functionality, and ease of managing consumption and Direct Debit payments.

author: Dorrie
body:
The graphs clearly show my usage over the 3 years is very similar each year. 
Surprised me, to be honest.
insight: User appreciates the app's ability to visualize long-term energy consumption patterns clearly.

author: batten
body:
Im poor on the internet (85!) but got there
However  app seems defunct not showing true readings
Particularly as  my late wife was on permanent oxygen  via concentrator and I wish to know deceease in usage since December Monthly update imperatve
insight: User reports the app is not displaying accurate meter readings, preventing them from monitoring energy usage changes since their wife's death.

author: Peter Smith
body: Staff were very helpful
insight: User had a positive experience with helpful staff support.

author: William Jolly
body: It’s the Scottish Power professional attitude and appearance that impresses me the most.
insight: User appreciates the professional demeanor and appearance of Scottish Power's staff or representatives.

author: Harry Colley
body: App worked fine for ages. About 2 weeks ago it would not let me log in. Everytime I need to top up I have to uninstall the app and the reinstall the app. It works for that log on and then I'm back to square 1.
insight: User experiences recurring login failures requiring repeated app reinstallation to access top-up functionality.

author: PBM Mason
body: can't get on to top up, very difficult to sign in can't use biometric anymore,keeps bouncing bk when reset password takes ages then when you go on again doesn't recognise PW again so all start again nightmare
insight: User is experiencing repeated authentication failures with biometric login disabled and password reset issues causing a frustrating login loop.

author: Mrs Rodica Oancea
body: They came back to me emailing quickly and helpful for me as a landlord.
insight: Customer appreciated quick and helpful email support regarding their landlord account.

author: Zelda Mozez
body: Good customer service
insight: User appreciated the quality of customer service provided by ScottishPower.

author: John
body: overal good but at peak times almost impossible to log on
insight: App performs well overall but experiences login accessibility issues during peak usage times.

author: Mick
body:
Whilst it's notoriously difficult to speak a human these days, the experience and support was excellent once I did get through to someone (Jen, I believe). 
Unfortunately, I have to call them back to request a refund (when it eventually shows on the system) as a glitch in the app ended with 2 payments going instead of 1.  So, I'm out of pocket this month.  Surely in this day and age there could be something in place to ensure hard working people can get their money back as quick as these organisations are able to take it. 🤔
insight: App billing glitch caused duplicate payment, but customer service quality was good once reached; user frustrated by slow refund process and difficulty reaching support.

author: Richard Clarke
body: It was time to change my fixed tariff. Easy to do through the app & quick to actually do the action needed. Like always it sometimes difficult to actually know exactly what you are buying into fully. Feel I've made the right decision, but can never be 100% sure. Overall though pleased with the transaction.
insight: User successfully switched tariffs via app but expresses uncertainty about tariff transparency and comparison options.

author: Susan Cade
body:
Scottish Power have been helpful with my circumstances but I am disappointed with changing to prepayment without being able to see when I need to put money in. 
I asked for details of where I lost so much money not keeping up with payments.
insight: Customer appreciates support but is frustrated by lack of visibility into prepayment balance and unclear communication about payment arrears.

author: Jatinder Meyon
body: all good
insight: User is satisfied with the app's overall performance and functionality.

author: MR C COLLINS
body:
i am not at all happy with Scottish Power due to my gas/electric meters going in reverse i.e  less than last readings for March. you must be sure that all meters are working correctly 100%. i am 95 yrs and am very wary of companys. yesterday i rang S P and spoke to a
advisor who in he next few days is going to try to help.
cannot remember his.  Christopher Collins 
have been with SCOT POWER SINCE 2011.
insight: Customer reports meters running in reverse showing lower readings than previous month and expresses concern about meter accuracy, though appreciates recent customer service outreach.

author: Anita anameje
body: App is the worst! I downloaded app less than a month ago and i have uninstall and installed three times already because it's not letting me login
insight: User is unable to log in and has reinstalled the app multiple times without resolving the authentication issue.

author: Angela
body: Given the current financial pressures, I am keen to keep track of my energy usage, but I have noticed a few occasions the information for usage on a particular day does not appear accurate, if, for example, I have done a load of washing and expect my electricity usage to be a little higher, that was not reflected on that particular day’s usage but appeared to be shown on the following day.
insight: Energy usage data displays with a one-day delay, failing to accurately reflect daily consumption patterns when usage spikes occur.

author: Dennis Ramsell
body:
Had to deal with a bereaved person's account.
Service was good, only problem was it took nearly 45min to complete on the phone.
Could be simpler and quicker.
insight: Customer service handled a sensitive bereaved account situation well, but the process was unnecessarily lengthy at 45 minutes and could be streamlined.

author: Muyiwa
body: Good. The gas meter cabinet is rusted and would appreciate it if you could replace it
insight: Customer reports rust damage to gas meter cabinet and requests replacement through the app.

author: Elizabeth
body: The agent on the phone was great. However the service up to that point was poor. As a landlord, we are responsible for the bills during empty periods. Our letting agent had passed you our postal address, but this never reached your billing dept which meant no bills were reaching us. When trying to phone the only option i was given was to key in my account number (which i didnt have as i hadnt had a bill). Holding on just produced the same request 3 times before eventually being put through to an actual (helpful) person. I had given up trying to get through on seversl previous occasions
insight: Customer struggled to contact support due to poor phone system design and address information not reaching billing department, though the agent ultimately reached was helpful.

author: Maria R
body: Easy to use app to keep on top of bills
insight: User appreciates the app's user-friendly interface for monitoring and managing their energy bills.

author: John Quinn
body: The staff member I spoke to was very helpful and knowledgeable couldn't have been better really. The problem as always is the time to get through and holding times.
insight: Staff quality is excellent but lengthy call waiting times remain a frustrating barrier to service access.

author: Jules
body:
I was with Scottish power for years but transferred just before my fixed rate ended with them. Octopus still had fixed rates so they said I could transfer with no penalties as within the timeframe. Scottish Power penalised me anyway and I had to pay £100 as they said I was 2 days out of the penalty timeframe?!?
I will never return to them for this reason as that just shows what type of company they are…..loyal customers get no special rates and then when you leave they penalise you.
insight: Customer was unexpectedly charged a £100 early exit penalty despite transferring within what they believed was the allowed timeframe, demonstrating poor customer retention practices and lack of loyalty rewards.

author: Janice-Peter James
body: I have no problems with Scottish Power as a power supplier but after 6 years our smart meter still isn’t functioning
insight: Customer is satisfied with the energy supply service but frustrated by a non-functional smart meter that has persisted for 6 years.

author: Richard Hedger
body: The agent was helpful and forwarded my request to the correct person; however, they have yet to respond, which seems to be par for the course.
insight: Agent provided good support but follow-up response times remain problematic and inconsistent.

author: Lee Turnbull
body: The new verification code process via email is a pain in the ass !
insight: User finds the new email-based verification code process for authentication inconvenient and frustrating.

author: Glenn
body: I like the instant readings enabling me to know my exact costs
insight: User appreciates the app's ability to provide real-time energy consumption data and cost transparency.

author: Shona Porteous
body: The app let's me keep on top of my energy usage and the half price weekends are a great help.
insight: User appreciates the app's energy monitoring capabilities and finds the half-price weekend tariff beneficial.

author: Sukhdev Saini & Miss Gurbachan
body: Prompt and professional service and support given by the customer service advisor.
insight: Customer praised the responsiveness and professionalism of the support team.

author: Tomasz Placek
body: My experience is super, everything is realy clear to understand and read.
insight: User praises the app's clarity and user-friendly interface design.

author: Colin
body: Very helpful when I was worrying about what I could done
insight: User appreciated helpful support that addressed their concerns about energy costs or usage.

author: Mr Worthington
body: The lasy got my supply back on really quickly, then I to thing I am not happy with is I can not access how much electricity I have got either on my smart meter or the main electricity box as that has got a pin on that does not work at all
insight: Customer praises quick service restoration but is frustrated by inability to access electricity usage data on their smart meter and main box due to a non-functional PIN system.

author: Keith Styles
body: Easy to understand app showing usage
insight: User appreciates the app's clear interface for monitoring energy consumption.

author: Mr Colin Ambrose
body: When Scottish Power have fixed the problem I will then be able to rate them. At the moment they are not worth any stars
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with an unresolved issue and cannot provide a fair rating until Scottish Power resolves the problem.

author: Nicholas Slabbert
body: easy payment system
insight: User appreciates the straightforward and user-friendly payment functionality of the app.

author: Steven Memory
body: scottish power is a good company never had any problems with them used them for a few years now.
insight: Long-term customer expresses satisfaction with ScottishPower's service and reliability.

author: Simon Orr
body:
Finding it all very complicated and needing to keep
A constant eye on the account
insight: User finds the account interface overly complicated and requires frequent monitoring to manage their energy account effectively.

author: Janice Fairlie
body: very easy app to use, calculates very quickly so you know what is going on in the account, I am very happy with it, thank you
insight: User praises the app's intuitive design and quick performance for account visibility.

author: James
body: Kept well informed on tariff options and easy to submit meter readings.
insight: User appreciates clear communication about tariff options and the convenience of submitting meter readings through the app.

author: Valerie Burton
body: Once we were able to get somebody to speak to us they were very helpful. For elderly people like myself & husband it is very difficult to contact anybody as you are told to go to 'this email address, etc'   At 83 & 87 years of age we are not into mobiles and computers.  We had to wait 3 days for our grandson to come and set us up with your team.  I now know that there was a problem with British Gas due to us living on a new build site.
insight: Staff were helpful once contact was made, but elderly customers struggle with digital-only contact channels and require significant assistance to access support.

author: Paul
body: The information provided is very clear
insight: User appreciates the clarity of information presented in the app despite giving a 4-star rather than 5-star rating.

author: Emma McPherson
body: Broken, can't login, I can login to the website but you can only top-up via the app not the website.
insight: User is frustrated by app login failures and the inability to top-up through the website, forcing reliance on a non-functional mobile app.

author: Mr richard smith
body: Would rather have half price at weekend
insight: User appreciates the service but would prefer discounted weekend energy rates.

author: Wendy
body: Have rarely had any problems
insight: User has had a generally reliable experience with few problems encountered.

author: Julia Oliveira
body: The worst company that exists. They charged me for 4 years of gas and electricity bills for flats that were not mine. Now I’ve discovered everything and I’m taking legal action against them. They put me under a lot of psychological pressure, with calls, letters, and emails demanding payment of bills in the name of “occupier.” They forced me to pay £8,000. I became ill and had extremely serious financial difficulties trying to pay this. Now we’ve discovered that none of these bills were mine, and after the complaint deadline passes, I will be taking them to court. DO NOT USE SCOTTISH POWER — THEY ARE NOT HONEST.
insight: Customer was incorrectly billed for 4 years for properties they did not occupy and subjected to aggressive collection tactics, causing significant financial and psychological harm.

author: ANITA H
body: On the whole they provide a good service and prices are usually competitive. Via the website or app, customers have control over their direct debit amount and there are useful tools to keep track of energy usage and compare to previous years. I had some issues last year , but they were sorted out eventually to my satisfaction. Recently they changed the half price electricity weekend deal to give customers more choice of time slots to save. Sounded good on the face of it but I think Scottish Power think customers won’t notice that they’ve reduced it from 10 hours at the weekend to 8 hours . Well I have noticed ! That said it was useful to be able to choose a better time to save. I hope they continue to give us the best prices when energy costs rise in July.
insight: Customer appreciates competitive pricing and digital tools but feels misled by the reduction in weekend electricity savings hours from 10 to 8 despite marketing it as expanded choice.

author: Arun
body: Not a good responsibility customer service,  making money only, not a good deal offers tariff telling ext..IMPORTANT very worst customer care agents. Always Rod speaks
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with poor customer service quality, unfavorable tariff offers, and feels the company prioritizes profit over customer care.

author: Carmen Hobbs
body: App worked fine for months. Now every time I go to top up I can't login. I have to uninstall then reinstall the app for it to work!
insight: User experiences repeated login failures requiring app reinstallation to restore functionality.

author: Graham Mundy
body: Very good experience
insight: User had a positive experience with the app overall but did not specify which features or services contributed to their satisfaction.

author: david bowns
body: good app
insight: User is satisfied with the overall functionality and performance of the ScottishPower app.

author: Sian Evans
body:
Avoid! We’re new to Scottish Power and asked them to set up our gas and electric account. They set up our gas but not electric.

 They then closed our gas account without telling us and opened an electric account. When they re-opened the gas account, they set it up wrong, we haven’t been able to top-up our gas and now being left without hot water or heating as they say they cannot top it up from there side. 

Awful customer service, they do not listen to you. There’s so much background noise, so you cannot hear them. 

They will happily let you go without hot water or heating. 

Moving to British Gas and already had a much better experience.
insight: Multiple account setup errors combined with poor customer service and communication failures left the customer without essential utilities.

author: LINDA DEAVES
body: Scottish power issue of not registering my electric meter on national grid caused me stress anxiety and time and energy when I was caring for both parents with dementia. I rang n rang n went to Venus women's support centre for their help to contact. I ended up 2 years with ombudsman deciding I owed over £2000. I am on benefits........then I had to ring up to get the 4 action points done by them. I finally got a smart meter n I have been paying off the debt for 2 years........terrible experience with them.
insight: Customer experienced severe distress due to unregistered meter causing a £2000 debt dispute that took two years to resolve through ombudsman, compounded by poor service responsiveness.

author: Lisa Nightingale
body: can never get on to account it's frustrating when you want to top up
insight: User is unable to access their account to top up credit, causing significant frustration.

author: Shelly James
body: I have to repeatedly uninstall and reinstall the app everytime i want to log in just to put electricity on my account.. its ridiculous
insight: User experiences repeated app crashes when attempting to log in and add credit to their electricity account.

author: Darren Marsden
body: Good service
insight: User is satisfied with the overall service provided by ScottishPower.

author: Kassandra Woods
body: half price electric ( not all the time 🤔😁) you can choose the suitable time for when you want it. , yes please to that I said. Smart meter is easy to use. I have a pay as you go meter and it's so easy to top it up-put the app on phone- top up from the comfort of my home. the only thing that does my head in is those electric box covers, the wind damages it- usually the door will come off and then it needs replacing, not everyone has the money to fix them! (I will still give 5*)
insight: Customer appreciates time-of-use pricing and app functionality but expresses concern about physical meter box cover durability and replacement costs.

author: Eyoel habtemariam
body: good
insight: User expresses overall satisfaction with the app despite providing minimal detail.

author: Siulun
body: Some good ,if can change the meter better
insight: User is generally satisfied with the app but would like improved smart meter functionality or replacement options.

author: Anthony Ashworth
body: I’ve been told the last 3 reviews someone would be in touch and my last review I was told someone would be in touch that day but my phone never rang ! What a surprise
insight: Customer has repeatedly been promised follow-up contact from representatives but calls never materialise, indicating poor communication and service delivery.

author: AGNES
body: The options went round in a loop.
insight: User experienced a frustrating navigation loop while trying to access app options.

author: James Hamilton
body: its a disgrace i havent been able to access the app for 7 weeks ive had numerous calls from scottish power each time telling me an expert will contact me and still waiting so ive had enough tommorrow i will be changing supplier
insight: User has been unable to access the app for 7 weeks with unresolved support tickets, leading to intent to switch suppliers.

author: MRS MIRIAM G GOSS
body: The attention and the outcome of my complaint.
insight: User is satisfied with how their complaint was handled and resolved.

author: Alan
body: got a phone call today about why I have posted a bad review about Scottish power. This the normal route they take ie: about 7 phone calls all about this problem with my wall box charger they sold me .they agreed as i had to wait a year for the ground works to complete by Scottish power networks so charger was only fitted for 2 years .I got so frustrated missing appointments cause the charger did not charge during the night ,as this is for a mobility car. I had to either use it as a dumb charger or it would not work at all so what they said is go to wall box and get a new charger fitted as they agreed it was still under warranty . By this time we are 2 years of complaining. wall box are- if you would believe anybody can top Scottish power for bad customer support even worse .the give generic computer responses about 3 to 4 day later to complaints and instruction on how to fix the rubbish wall box which do not work. So i ripped the wall box off the wall in frustration as i had to use public charging again. Got a  hyper volt charger fitted at my cost which works as advertised now Scottish power are saying because I've lost patience with them and wall box  they are not going to refund me ,they should refund me for a product they sold me not fit for purpose and compensate me for the years of pubic charging and frustration and stress the have caused me. Update they phoned me today with no resolution no refund no way forward and are now claiming the charger is out of warranty -seriously ???.WHAT A PAIR OF GREAT COMPANYS BUYER BEWARE ?not a chance of money back for Scottish power-like their power it only flows one way and have no regard for their customers so now I have to go to citizens advice and my local member of parliament as no ombudsman in place for this complaint. I will post bad reviews forever   about this as i feel I have been scammed by Scottish power.update on Jodie answer they have said they can't and will not help me !????
insight: Customer experienced prolonged product failure and poor support from both ScottishPower and Wallbox, ultimately refusing refund for non-functional EV charger sold as fit for purpose.

author: Elise Sullivan
body: never lets you log in! waste of time
insight: User is unable to access the app due to persistent login failures.

author: Patrick Clipperton
body: I've struggled to access the web links and phone number but since adopting SP power supplies I have been trying to tailor my usage with your "reduced " cost offer. I have finally sent an email but so far no response. Can someone please pick up my messages and respond.
insight: Customer is unable to access contact information and has not received a response to their email inquiry about a reduced cost offer.

author: Name
body: I've had to reinstall the app every time I want to use it
insight: The app requires reinstallation before each use, indicating a critical stability or persistence issue.

author: Emma Clark
body: can't see how to top up or credit
insight: User is unable to locate the top-up or credit functionality within the app, indicating a usability or navigation issue.

author: Phyllis Jesson
body: The lady I spoke to was very helpful and informing, but I think it is disgusting to raise my monthly direct debit an extra £55.00 a month which is £660.00 per annum when I live on my own, being a widow, on a state pension and after yesterday’s payment only owe you £198.00!!!!!
insight: Customer praises helpful service staff but is extremely dissatisfied with a £55 monthly direct debit increase despite having a low outstanding balance of £198.

author: Dave Kenyon
body: The app is good. Slightly confusing in some parts and a bit slow but tops up the account, and is quite informative.
insight: App successfully handles account top-ups and provides useful information, but suffers from minor usability and performance issues.

author: Paul Missen
body: come on Scottish power get the app sorted out it doesn't work
insight: User is experiencing critical app functionality issues that prevent them from using the service.

author: Sheila Williams
body: would give this app no stars if I could it's horrendous, keeps saying log in details wrong after a couple of days even tried uninstalling and installing again and same thing happens, thinking of changing suppliers that fed up with it
insight: User is experiencing persistent login authentication failures even after reinstalling the app, leading to consideration of switching suppliers.

author: Reginald Crouch
body: Good professional staff polite and knowledgeable
insight: User praises the professionalism, politeness, and knowledge of the staff.

author: Philip Pinchin
body: Ease of use.
insight: User appreciates the application's user-friendly interface and straightforward navigation.

author: Olatunbosun Koyi
body: The agent that attended to me was very pleasant and accommodating. Definitely she knows her job without any compromise.
insight: User praises the competence and pleasant demeanor of a customer service agent who effectively resolved their issue.

author: Julie Taylor
body: its not good you cant get into your account madness you do everything still cant get in how on earth are u going to top up
insight: User is unable to access their account despite multiple login attempts, preventing them from topping up their energy credit.

author: SADIA
body: Useless customer service, no point in ever complaining as they'll never call you back
insight: Customer is frustrated with unresponsive customer service and lack of follow-up on complaints.

author: PAUL
body:
Once I finally got through to the advisor she was very helpful and arranged an appointment to replace my dead gas meter. Unfortunately an hour after the call, I was informed via text, that the appointment was cancelled.
This has resulted in me going through the entire process again today to be told there aren’t any appointments for the foreseeable future and I will receive an email at some point to inform me when they are coming.
insight: Customer experienced poor appointment scheduling with cancellations and long wait times for meter replacement despite helpful advisor support.

author: Sean
body: Never ever use this company
insight: User expresses strong dissatisfaction with ScottishPower and advises against using the company, though specific issues are not detailed.

author: susan magowan
body:
I was pleased to receive the email daily promptly explaining that is was a yearly bill. I was not pleased however to receive another email reminding
me to pay the extra bill
insight: User appreciated timely annual bill notification but was frustrated by a subsequent payment reminder email.

author: customer FIAZ AHMED
body: Good service
insight: User is satisfied with the overall service quality provided by ScottishPower.

author: Kenneth
body: Very helpful with my problem.  Pleasant and cheerful.
insight: User praised the helpfulness and pleasant demeanor of customer service staff when resolving their issue.

author: frequent user
body:
Bills are being issued in the Welsh language - never requested.

We have a type 1 smart meter  which, we think, provides meter readings to Scottish Power, or its agents, every 30 minutes or so. It does not communicate with the household unit so we can’t monitor our power useage - this issue has been flagged for 2 years and once again has been passed to the technical department who have been tasked with contacting us within 7 days of our discussion with customer services.
insight: Customer frustrated by unsolicited Welsh language bills and a two-year unresolved smart meter communication issue with poor follow-up from technical support.

author: Nisar Lone
body: Taking advantage of me being an old 75 man with dementia and a stroke. Just dodging my calls and messages on the app
insight: Customer reports being ignored through calls and app messages while experiencing vulnerability due to age and health condition.

author: Paul
body: I’ve paid the Final invoice. You have now hounded me not once, but twice more now.
insight: Customer is frustrated by repeated payment collection attempts after already settling their final invoice.

author: BurningGodzilla
body: can never log in its stupid
insight: User is unable to log into the app and expresses frustration with the authentication functionality.

author: Andrew Dunsire
body: If you are considering using Scottish Power for charging your electric vehicle I suggest you read this debacle. On setting up an EV tariff in January 2025 to take advantage of the off peak rate between midnight and 5 in the morning. After a few months the bill I received showed an enormous amount of electricity consumed and was obviously incorrect. Subsequently my bills look incorrect with the proportion of off peak and peak consumption not agreeing to my meter readings in the house. My ScottishPower App is showing consumption in the wrong half hour slots. We are now a year or so on and I just get passed from pillar to post. The information we need to unravel this I am told as it will “overload the system “.I have spent days of my time on this and have no confidence that it will get sorted. I believe I have to reach deadlock before I take the matter to the Small Claims Court and Ofgen.
insight: Customer's EV tariff billing and meter readings have been consistently incorrect for over a year, with the app displaying wrong consumption data and support staff unable or unwilling to resolve the issue.

author: Charlotte Lovell
body: LETS NOT GO THERE , COSTS TOO MUCH !
insight: User is dissatisfied with high energy costs and pricing.

author: customer
body: Very helpful and sorted my problem.
insight: Customer appreciates helpful support that resolved their issue quickly.

author: Jo Pollard
body: Agent able to read smart meter as meter wasnt sending readings .Lets hope this resolves the problem going forward .
insight: Customer service agent successfully resolved a smart meter reading issue, though the user remains cautiously optimistic about the long-term fix.

author: Naomi Failes
body: awful
insight: User expresses extreme dissatisfaction with the app or service but provides no specific details about the underlying problem.

author: Mrs Evendon
body: Problems with a new smart meter installed in November 2026 after the previous one failed. Since then I receive no information on the app/online account for gas usage. One day they blame the app, the next day I’m assured that all app issues have been resolved. When I request a visit to check that the smart meter is working correctly they tell me that it is and it must be a problem with the app. Four plus months of this nonsense and the merry go round continues!!!

author: Temumad
body:
Despite emails and chats saying the totals don’t add up for usage,  
No readings at all some days then same usage reading 3 days the same. I have no in-house reader as mine was faulty and have been told they no longer give them out, I thought this was the point of having a smart meter!!!!
insight: Customer reports inconsistent usage readings and lack of physical smart meter support, questioning the value of their smart meter service.

author: J W
body: Are these positive reviews real/genuine? I find this difficult to believe that this is the same company that I’ve been trying to resolve an issue with since November!  One member of complaints team lied that he’d sorted this out at their end only to find this was not resolved and this has now apparently re-opened as new complaint and somebody was going to be in touch and that was over a week ago and heard nothing since! Will now leave in hands of Ombudsman/regulator who can hold them to account.
insight: Customer is extremely frustrated with unresolved complaints mishandled by the complaints team over several months, leading to escalation to the ombudsman.

author: Samantha Robinson
body: EDIT..... tried uninstalling and reinstalled app. managed to get logged in but wouldn't let me top up without putting the long UTRN number in manually. logged me back out and did the same thing again. The app has not been working. I can't log in at all. I can't top up. I can't contact anyone via the app. emailing is useless as they wait 5 working days to get back to you. please get it sorted. I need to top up and cant as its nearly 10pm and I cant get on my app !!!!

author: Karen Palacio
body: Easy to follow instructions online
insight: User appreciates the clarity and ease of online instructions for managing their account.

author: Rita Zadi
body:
Speechless. After more than a year of disputes, I’ve ended up with a credit mark and a debt collector involved—yet still no clear explanation, no proper resolution, and no accountability.

My winter reduction payment was received, but it has never appeared on my account. Despite raising this multiple times, nothing has been corrected.

To make matters worse, when I tried to pay the debt collector, the payment failed twice due to “technical issues.” On top of that, the amount requested by Richburn differs from what was shown on my original SP account—lower, but unexplained. This inconsistency raises serious concerns.

I have kept all evidence throughout this process. From my experience, this does not feel like simple error or mismanagement—it feels like a system that allows people to be misled and financially affected without proper oversight.

This situation has caused unnecessary stress and damage to my credit record. I expected professionalism, transparency, and basic accountability—none of which have been delivered.

I strongly urge this to be investigated and taken seriously so others do not go through the same experience.
insight: Customer experienced prolonged billing disputes with a missing payment, debt collector involvement, payment processing failures, and inconsistent debt amounts, with no resolution or accountability despite multiple escalations.

author: Miranda
body: Well treated
insight: User is satisfied with the quality of customer service received from ScottishPower.

From an experience i can say they have the worst customer service ever

They refuse to let me speak to the managers and keep telling me to end the line then call again if i wanted to speak to another agent. Mind you i’m on their priority service.

author: Isobel campbell
body:
I think quite possibly the worst services I have received in a long time. 

I am a young first time home owner, and as everybody knows how stressful moving and everything with that is, Scottish Power did not help this at all. 

They first and foremost cut our supply for over 24 hours with no help and no mention of sending out an engineer. 

 I called 9 times, and 3 of them times I was meant to receive a call back which I did not. 

Now I have figured it out my own enquiry on my own. After days of dealing with this company. 

I never leave negative reviews, however how the staff has dealt with my service and any issues I have had has been ridiculous and quite frankly disgusting.

Don't even think about replying with your "sorry for the experience" script—save your breath. You’ve had a year to fix this and failed. I’m moving back to EDF energy today. Do not contact me ever again... I should have never left EDF Energy...

insight: User is frustrated that frequent app updates introduce bugs and stability issues, contrasting ScottishPower's performance negatively against independent developers.

body: Been with Scottish power for a long time so think review speaks for itself
insight: Long-term customer expressing general satisfaction with ScottishPower's service through their loyalty.

author: Stephen Leggate
body: I have both gas and electric from Scottish Power. I have a smart meter for gas which works perfectly. However, after a failed attempt to set up a smart meter for electricity 5 years ago I am no further forward getting one installed. Despite being bombarded on a daily basis by adverts on TV urging everyone to get a smart meter, and making frequent requests,Scottish Power have made no attempt to supply one. In fact, to add insult to injury, I recently received an email congratulating me on getting one. Scottish Power have not been near my house and I don't have a smart meter. Something is far wrong with the system. Scottish Power need to do better.
insight: Customer has been unable to get an electricity smart meter installed despite repeated requests over 5 years and received false confirmation of installation.

author: melvyn henderson
body: Energy supplier with questionable billing and accounting. Open and close accounts on a prepaid meter with false debt. Still same practice after smart meter installed in May 2025. author: Brandon McConnell
body: Terrible customer service over a year I’ve chased them up about a debt they put on my credit file when calling up there was no record of this. 1 year in and I’ve been calling weekly emailing daily and still at the same stage. Constant promises that you will hear back that day and not once over a minimum of 10 promises has this happened. Now having to work with the energy ombudsman and today started proceedings with the courts. The stress and hassle this has caused me for over a year and as mentioned nothing changes. I’m now left with no option but to cost myself money to take this to a court and solicitor.
insight: Customer has spent over a year trying to resolve a disputed debt on their credit file with no progress despite repeated contact attempts and broken promises from ScottishPower, forcing them to escalate to the ombudsman and courts.

author: S Morningstar
body: cant log in, try to reset my password and it says the email link has already been used! what's going on!?!?!
insight: User is unable to access their account due to a password reset link that cannot be reused, causing frustration and locked-out status.

author: Owen
body:
Ive had The same issue since December its now 30th March, all I needed to do was change direct debit dates, this would normally be straightforward of a job for any company except Scottish power.
Ive been on the phone a total of 8 different times and each phone call lasting over an hour to do this. I wanted to change from the 6th of each month to the 21st of each month due to changing jobs and the pay dates were different.
I thought this was sorted after the first 2 conversations but they went on to take payments on the 14th and then the 16th then after more conversations and more people telling me they have 100% sorted it they just didnt take payment at all. The amount of stress this has caused me has 100% made me realise I will never refer anyone to Scottish power, you could pardon a mistake once of twice due to the language barrier that was in hand but this much hassle for a simple objective is bone idle and lack of care from customer services. 
Then the last lady I spoke to she was worth her weight in gold, finally someone at Scottish power who cared, she didn't only manage to do the "impossible" of changing a direct debit date but she also managed to find a fault with my gas meter she jumped through all the hoops and processes to sort it out for me and got it sorted and scheduled a follow-up call to see if 1, the payment come out on the chosen date and 2, if the gas meter is up and running still. If Scottish power had more of that lady working with them they would have a long list of happy customers and achieve great things, instead ive had to go through multiple people who didn't care about me as a customer and had absolutely no enthusiasm towards what job they were clocked in to do and get paid for.
insight: Customer experienced a four-month struggle to change direct debit dates due to repeated staff errors and poor service, though one exceptional agent ultimately resolved the issue and identified a gas meter fault.

author: James Rutherford
body: Used to be great, then suddenly my bio sign on just disappeared, it's not my phone I have checked everyhthing, the installed app, cleared cache, the registered sign on. next day it's gone again. Really disappointed now.
insight: User's biometric sign-on feature repeatedly fails despite troubleshooting attempts, causing frustration with the app's reliability.

author: Stephen Clarke
body: Keeping my gass and electric bills in one place .easier for me
insight: User appreciates the convenience of managing gas and electricity bills through a single consolidated platform.

author: Gman Can
body:
If I could give less than 1 star then I would.

They are breathtakingly incompetent.

In the past 3 years I have spent a total of more than 6 hours on the phone trying to resolve an issue which is entirely caused by Scottish Power. The call centre staff are generally very pleasant and usually helpful. I get assured repeatedly that they understand the issue and they say that it is all written in the file for the next person to see but mostly that does not happen. I get further calls but the caller cannot see that I have been told the issue is resolved (It has not been). The cycle of confusion continues....just rinse and repeat, as it were. The final straw came today when I was contacted by solicitors chasing a debt but they had been instructed the same day that Scottish Power last assured me that there was no problem.

The Scottish Power account is in the name of my elderly sister who has dementia and Alzheimers but lives alone. She is on the S.P. VUlnerable Person list (but keeps getting removed from that). I have authorisation to deal with S.P. and hold a power of attorney. The customer has a direct debit arrangement in place and I have been repeatedly told by S.P. on the phone that they are happy and the account details are correct BUT when they issue bills, the bills say on them that the agreed amount will be taken on a particular date. The invoices say clearly "you do not need to do anything further". S.P. then fails to take the direct debit (despite assurances that they will) and so the debt builds up. I have repeatedly been told that the problem is solved. On 23rd March £588 was paid on a debit card at the request of S.P. who also agreed a new monthly direct debit amount to clear the outstanding debt....at an amount higher than what they were suggesting. The customer does not dispute the bills and is quite willing to pay by the direct debit that S.P. holds.....however, S.P. just fails to take the money owing and it is not the fault of the customer or her bank. So I believed that the matter was resolved by S.P. on 23rd of March when I spoke several times to a woman called Ndombe at their call centre in South Africa who totally assured me thaT Scottish Power was now happy and it had been all their fault. Later that  same day S.P. instructed Flint Bishop Solicitors to persue the customer despite telling me that the issue was resolved and had been the fault of Scottish Power. If this matter continues, I will not rest until it has been aired in the press and on TV consumer shows.
insight: Systemic failures in direct debit processing and internal communication over three years, compounded by inconsistent vulnerable person support and contradictory debt collection actions, have caused severe frustration and financial distress.

author: JOSEPH MAWERE Opoku
body: Easy and fast
insight: User appreciates the app's speed and ease of use.

author: Beth McCrossan

author: A Google user
body: Alls good while things work OK but if anything goes wrong it's a nightmare to get anything sorted out. Our gas meter failed last November and it's April this week and still waiting to sort the account out. Hopeless. Moving to British gas when it's sorted

body: Prices I was expecting a little bit lower with a EV charger?

author: Alex
body:

Instead of explaining how much I should expect to pay, they have provided a number of links for you to do your own research. Talk about making it complicated and making me feel that I could be charged whatever they want because it's not clearly explained.

I definitely do not recommend Scottish power and I just wish I had done more research before signing up with them. 
Just to add, I'm a business user on a fixed tariff

author: Micheal Smyth

author: Hafeez Rehman
body:
Switched my Dual account to Scottish power after asking the support team if my vehicle is eligible for anytime charge, which I was confirmed. Then checked on Scottish power Eligiblity checker multiple times and answer was eligible. So went ahead and switched accounts one by one, switching electric was another storey as was told, they already have an active Scottish power Electric account on my address, strange enough as I have been with Octopus since last 6 years. After about 5 calls that was sorted.

Now I have been told Scottish can't help me as they are unable to connect my vehicle on anytime change rate and been advised to take 12-5am saver Charging offer, in this process I have wasted a week and x 3 money to Scottish on charging in comparison to what I was paying to Octopus. So feeling clearly been let down by dishonest people. 

Based on my experience I will advise, Please double check that you are getting what you have been promised if you are switching to Scottish Power.
Thanks
insight: Customer experienced significant service failures and misleading eligibility information when switching to ScottishPower's EV charging rates, resulting in financial loss and unmet promises.

author: Abysmal Abysmal

author: A M
body:
This app has caused a lot of trouble for our family. Using the same email address and the same home address, it created two separate accounts for me (it might have been due to something I did, but I’m not sure what went wrong).

Another issue is that my wife wanted to access our account on her phone, but she couldn’t. It seems she would need to log in using my email on her device, which is very inconvenient. We haven’t even tried further because the process is too complicated.

It has been almost a month since we opened the account, and my account is still not functioning properly.

author: Jason Attfield

author: Hayley Spicer

author: sunnie Odwyer
body: From the start of me switching over I've had nothing but issues. I paid my £124 each month, i had £298 in my balance account, so I asked for a refund if your not using my account and then just decided to take £250 i asked why have you taken this, because I haven't give a meter reading, so I gzve meter readings and got £60 back, I will never recommend Scottish power to anyone and they still haven't give me a good reason why I apparently used all this gas and electric
insight: Customer is frustrated with unexplained charges and withdrawals from their account, and feels they have not received adequate explanation or support from ScottishPower.

author: Jamie Lievesley
body: As with many others, I'm also not able to login, just get a login loop and error. I "upgraded" to a smart meter recently and now I can't top up at all! Terrible experience. I'm going to run out of power. The online support is useless, stuck behind a bot that can't answer basic questions, finally get it to connect me to a real human and turns out they're all busy

author: Michael Gendon
body:
I would give zero if possible but it’s not.

I received an email from Scottish Power regarding smart meters.

They obviously have not heard of the lie of omission. They say about dispelling myths. What they do not say is that smart meters enables them to remotely cut you off without any justification.

How can I trust a company like that when they have increased my bill whilst they recognise that they owe me even more than last year and refuse to refund.

Do not trust them. The unusually high amount of five stars reviews indicate so called AI bots paid for by Scottish Power.

Added as a reply to Scottish Power. The problem is your reply is an actual lie. I have to go through hoops and argue to get any refunds and that’s after waiting 30 minutes to an hour for an answer of a phone call. And then wait a month for a repayment.

As for cutting people off no one believes that you have not cut anyone off who has switched to electronic meter readers. As there has been a high level of complaints regarding your company and cutting people off.
insight: Customer distrust of ScottishPower stems from concerns about undisclosed smart meter remote disconnection capabilities, unresolved billing disputes, and difficult refund processes.

author: Mr Nigel Dixon
body:
The advisor I eventually got to talk to was very helpful and professional. I wanted to find out my rates and thought it would be on my account, NO!
I then went for frustrating chat with a bot###
I asked to be out through to a human, all agents are busy! Very frustrating to round in circles. Eventually I phoned and got holed of the agent and blood pressure went down.
insight: User experienced significant frustration navigating the app and chatbot before finally reaching a helpful human agent by phone.

author: Tara C
body: I've had so many issues lately with this app. I can't even log in now to manage my balance or top up, I get stuck in a log in loop and get a bland error message. As I'm disabled, I rely on this to be able to top up my pre pay meters, so my gas and electricity doesn't get shut off. I reached out to the support team and they just said they're aware of customers having issues.
