author: stuart jenkins
body: can't log into the app. keep trying to reset password and the reset email says already used. can't top up my account this app is broken.
insight: User is unable to access their account due to a broken password reset function, preventing them from topping up their prepayment balance.

author: Steven Varley
body:
Reasonable prices.
Power save credit
insight: Customer appreciates the competitive pricing and Power Save Credit rewards program offered by ScottishPower.

author: Lisa Dudley
body: I am currently a PAYG customer through choice as I wanted to see what I was spending, I have had a smart meter fitted after 2 failed appointments, I would like to find a better tariff but the App has no information or links and the automated chat is poor
insight: Customer frustrated by poor app functionality for tariff comparison and weak automated support, despite successfully obtaining a smart meter after multiple failed appointments.

author: Amber Dorn
body: Terrible app. can never log in, system always seems to be down so switching
insight: User is unable to access the app due to persistent login failures and system downtime, prompting them to switch providers.

author: Francisco De Melo
body: Excellent service
insight: User is satisfied with the overall service quality provided by ScottishPower.

author: Don't  Like reviews
body: Recently they increased their marketing campaign. Even called my daughter who lives temporarily with us. We also received a letter with someone’s name on it but with our address.
insight: Customer is frustrated by aggressive marketing tactics and data handling issues, including unsolicited calls to family members and letters addressed to wrong names.

author: Melisha Ryan
body: Terrible functionality. The app shuts down periodically, logging me out multiple times with trouble logging back in even with biometrics. Tried to change the password but says link has already been used although it hadn't. It's really stressful sometimes trying to keep up with topping up the metre and monitoring credit, top up history ect... Seriously contemplating leaving Scottish Power due to these ongoing issues
insight: Multiple technical failures including app crashes, authentication issues, and password reset problems are causing user frustration and threatening customer retention.

author: Unliterate internet user
body:
Why do you keep advising me to have a Smart Meter when the Engineer has been to assess the situation and advised me that it is not possible to fit one!! Second review of the day. Why are we getting all these stupid games and jigsaws. All I want to do is put my meter readings in. It was much easier to do on the old version. As an 80 year old women, I found the previous version easy. This updated version is overly complicated. I only want to put my metre readings in and can’t get to the correct page.

Meter Readings

The next meterreading is due and I haven’t changed my opinion. This new version is a complete mystery to me. How do you put your meter readings in? I’ve pressed every button but no good.
I’m afraid, I’m going to have to change suppliers. I can’t put up with this every three or four months. It was so easy before. Now it’s  just confusion. 
Geraldine Bruton Customer
insight: Elderly user finds the app redesign confusing and unusable for submitting meter readings, and is considering switching suppliers due to repeated friction.

author: Stpauls
body: Great company to deal with, been with them for many years. I trust them, great communicators.
insight: Long-standing customer highly satisfied with company reliability, communication, and trustworthiness.

author: Peter Oltersdorf (PeterO)
body: Not been able to sign in for weeks now. Always get: something went wrong. It's infuriating.!!!
insight: User has been unable to access their account for weeks due to persistent login errors.

author: Jeanette Beasley
body: ever since the last update I have not been able to login. I have even tried uninstalling and reinstalling it and still dont work
insight: User is unable to log in after a recent app update despite attempting reinstallation.

author: Philip Milmine
body: For some reason, although our house is less than 18 months old and has smart meters for gas and electricity, we have to send meter readings for the gas every month. SP do offer times of the day with discounts of up to 50% so we try to plan around the times we have selected to turn on washing machine and dishwasher
insight: User frustrated that smart meters aren't functioning as expected, requiring manual gas meter readings despite having automated technology installed.

author: Margaret Cormie
body: Staff member very patient.
insight: User praised a staff member for their patience and helpful demeanor during an interaction.

author: Dritan Qosja
body: Good costumer service emazing
insight: User praises the quality of customer service despite minor spelling errors in their review.

author: David Fane
body: Good experience and easy to use
insight: User finds the app straightforward and pleasant to use overall.

author: ihate google
body: Awful app. "Oops something went wrong" continuously with no explanation.
insight: The app is experiencing persistent crashes with unhelpful error messages that prevent normal usage.

author: Alison H
body: hopeless! as so many have said, since latest update login is now a round and round issue of emails and used links and correct passwords being marked incorrect. Eventually after numerous re-installs and jumping through hoops of sending myself a link to prove I'm me 4 times I eventually got in to top up then it kicked me right back out. Its bad enough that the app balance is never up to date but now its rare if it will actually log in! Please sort it out. we pay for a system that works
insight: User is experiencing repeated authentication failures and account balance display issues following the latest app update, making the app unusable.

author: Mr Pervez Akhtar
body: It's good company I suppose it can't control as someone else is controlling increase in prices
insight: Customer acknowledges the company's competence but expresses frustration about price increases beyond the company's control.

author: Anne Matthews (Shaz)
body: cannot get on
insight: User is unable to access the app due to login issues.

author: DAVID FRENCH
body:
DO NOT HAVE SMART METERS
i have moved home trying to sort final bill
Smart meters gas and electric dont work
Have £580 ESTIMATED BILL no care no admittance the rubbish meters dont work credit history affected because they blame me for not paying.
SCOTTISH POWER ARE USELESS !!
Waiting standard 56 days to go to ombudsman to sort as they wont JOKE JOKE JOKE
insight: Customer is disputing an estimated bill after smart meters malfunctioned, feels ignored by ScottishPower, and is frustrated with the slow ombudsman escalation process.

author: Mr justin stone
body: Expensive and out of date technology.
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with high pricing and perceives the app as technologically outdated.

author: Mr THOMAS  OHARE
body:
I RECENTLY TRANSFERRED FROM ANOTHER SUPPLIER.
IT AL WENT VERY SMOOTHLY,
NO PROBLEMS WITH SCOTTISH GAS
insight: Customer had a smooth transfer experience from another supplier with no issues during the switching process.

author: Susan Horseman
body: keep getting logged out can't get back in. hopeless
insight: User is repeatedly logged out and unable to regain access to their account.

author: M D  Deol
body: Have been customer for more than 24 years but maybe it’s time to shop around as the units price for electric and gas is not competitive
insight: Long-standing customer considering switching due to uncompetitive pricing on electricity and gas units.

author: Harv
body: I found the app easy to navigate
insight: User appreciates the intuitive design and ease of navigation within the app interface.

author: SP
body: Takes a long time for app to open especially when checking your daily isage
insight: App has significant performance issues with slow loading times when accessing daily usage data.

author: KEN POOLE
body: Good. App
insight: User expresses satisfaction with the overall app functionality.

author: Anthony Ashworth
body: I’ve moved house in December and we had twin meters due to having storage heating but had them removed but Scottish power can’t seem to take me off the twin meters so I’m paying way too much during the day for my Electric. I phone them on a weekly basis I’ve made a complaint and I get text to say you have arranged a call and we will call you between certain times but nobody calls so I then call them and they promise someone will call that day as I explain it’s really messing with my mental health as we can’t afford the high rate electricity but nobody calls I can’t explain how fustrating this is but they just don’t seem know what to do so they just ignore me I can’t move supplier untill I’m off this twin meters which is making matters worse . Worst company I’ve ever dealt with we are now wishing we hadn’t moved !!
insight: Customer trapped on incorrect dual meter tariff with repeated failed promises for resolution calls, causing financial and mental health distress.

author: Bengaly Sano
body: please activate the app. my electric is almost off and I can't top up
insight: User is unable to top up their prepaid electricity account because the app is not functioning.

author: C Whitehouse
body: Fed up of not being able to pay my bill on line due to your app or sometimes the banks app saying. Ooooops something went wrong. X
insight: User is frustrated by recurring payment failures caused by app or bank errors when attempting to pay bills online.

author: Sophie Sass
body: Completely broken. Logged me out, now won't let me log in. email for password reset says the link has already been used, it hasn't. Gas rarely tops up for days when app is working. App doesn't seem to communicate with the meter or the meter isn't communicating with the app. Generally, pretty unreliable app.
insight: User is unable to log in due to a faulty password reset mechanism and experiences unreliable app-to-meter communication for gas top-ups.

author: Allan Thomson
body: good when it works.
insight: The app functions adequately when operational, but reliability issues suggest intermittent crashes or performance problems.

author: AM
body: not working
insight: The app is not functioning properly, preventing the user from accessing services.

author: Lisa Marshall
body: app not working.cant top up
insight: User is unable to top up their account due to the app malfunctioning.

author: MRFRANKDALBY
body: It took to long on my phone with the problem
insight: User experienced slow app performance when trying to resolve a problem on their phone.

author: Sarah Jane
body: getting on my nerves! need to top up and it won't let me log in!
insight: User is frustrated because they cannot log in to the app when attempting to top up their account.

author: John P
body: Not working and unable to add cash to my balance. I have medication in my fridge that I need to survive
insight: User cannot add credit to their prepaid account via the app and is concerned about loss of power affecting essential medical equipment.

author: Dorothy Potter
body: I find it easy to read and send my meter readings. I like the way my app works and easy to navigate.
insight: User appreciates the app's ease of use for submitting meter readings and navigating the interface.

author: NicWalt14
body: Good supply but won’t fit a smart meter
insight: Customer is satisfied with the energy supply service but frustrated by inability to install a smart meter.

author: DAVID WALKER
body: You keeping me up to date.
insight: User appreciates being kept informed and updated by the app.

author: Neil Cook
body: Frusting. Think I need to switch supplier. You can't top up when you need to. This app has been down for ages and it doesn't seem like a priority to fix it
insight: The app has been unavailable for an extended period, preventing users from topping up their prepaid balance when needed.

author: DerekC
body: Over charging on monthly direct debit. Phoned but computer says no - monthly use £15 asking for over £50 direct debit even though account is over £200 in credit. Finally after 4 more months of £15 usage got a refund of £100 but had to agree to £29 direct debit. Still over £150 in credit - useless system !
insight: Customer was overcharged on direct debit despite low usage and account credit, and faced poor resolution requiring multiple contacts over four months.

author: Kathleen
body: I missed a payment. But I had rung customer services to explain why I would  not be able to pay. I said I would ring up to make payment. The reason I could not make payment was because I became a pensioner and my date of payment was moved. After I explained this I was told to cancel my direct debit. After the initial phone call I received a total of 4 calls each one telling me I had missed a payment. One call was from a woman. She wasn't interested in an explanation, she told me she was going to put my payments up and I would have no choice she was going to put a prepayment metre in. I am not sure what date this call took place.
insight: Customer experienced poor service recovery after explaining payment difficulty, receiving multiple unwanted collection calls and threats of forced meter installation despite prior arrangement with customer services.

author: karen lawie
body: if I could i wouldnt even give it one star , havnt been able to log in for over a week now , ive tried password rest and it sends the link to do it but when you click on the link it says this link has expired and then the app just sends you on a loop jumping from page to page
insight: User is unable to access their account due to an expired password reset link that creates a navigation loop in the app.

author: Elaine Winstanley
body: The App allows me to see how much energy I am using.
insight: User appreciates the app's ability to provide real-time energy consumption visibility.

author: Rob Atlas
body: Absolutely useless, can't login to top up.
insight: User is unable to log in to the app to top up their account balance.

author: steve hale
body: never works when you need it the most !
insight: User experiences critical app failures during peak usage times when they most need the service.

author: Tara Godin
body:
Will my nightmare with Scottish power ever end?! 
Spent hours on the phone settling a bill for months, which was wrong as it was based on estimates rather than current usage. On settling the bill, I was assured by staff that my account had been closed. 
Today, I received a text message that a bill for £0.83 will be with me shortly. I absolutely can not believe this. I’ve lost so much energy going through costumer service team. Beyond exasperated.
insight: Customer experienced prolonged billing issues based on estimates, received conflicting account closure confirmation, and continues to receive unwanted bills despite resolution attempts.

author: Karen Hallam
body:
Honestly this company! 🤬
Received a call on Friday 27/3, a week after my supply was swapped to Eon, to say they were sorry that I was due to leave them… erm… I’d already left and they didn’t know?! Then I told her I’d previously made a complaint, which she had no knowledge of, proceeding to ask me many times whether it was my meter or the smart meter that was faulty as they’re split into 2 parts - something that she should have been able to see from the engineer’s report from the week before. I don’t know which cowing bit of it is faulty because I’m not a bloody engineer!!! 🤬
You’ll get my settlement of £24.58 for my gas bill by April 7th and that will be me done with you!
Danial already agreed to zero my electricity account when my meter is fixed by Eon, so I’ll owe you nothing once the gas is paid. 
Also, I didn’t agree to close the complaint, it was closed by SP because I was switching supplier. 

I’m actually very happy to be with Eon now, as their Next Smart Saver Fixed 12m tariff gives me 3 hours of half price electricity every day, and a further 19 hours every day at a vastly reduced price!! HUGE SAVINGS!!
insight: Customer experienced poor service during account closure including lack of knowledge of previous complaints and inability to provide clear information about a faulty meter issue.

author: Miss Susan Meah
body: I am happy with SP and will remain with them, I liked the half price weekend usage.
insight: Customer is satisfied with ScottishPower and appreciates the half-price weekend usage pricing offer.

author: Tracie Tasker
body: i cant log in i cant get gas or electric because i don't have the bar codes now absolutely useless, just one big loop i cant top up
insight: Customer cannot access their account or top up their energy credit due to missing barcodes and a confusing app interface.

author: J Tay
body:
Disgusting company thats got everything wrong from the moment I switched.
Upon switching the app failed to let me top up or see my usage or balance, they added credit to my account but didn't state I'd need to pay the electric back, not the gas which is weird.
Now 2 months later I have been logged out the app, the password that works on the website doesn't work. I've uninstall it, reinstalled it. Done forgot password, done password reset, done temporary password and been in the online support chat for an hour and 20 to be told try logging back in 24 hours and goto the shop to top up.
So now with a smart meter while it's snowing i have to manually get top ups and enter them into my meters because the support staff ignored my request to have £10 added to both my electric and gas amd switching to on supply and not prepayment takes around 3 days & not advised to do. Great help!

Do not join this useless company, if their app stops working there is no telephone support at the weekend, omar from whatever 3rd world country who types slow because he only has 2 working fingers is no use & neither was the other support staff member. They take around 10 minutes to answer a question ffs..

You cannot see your balance on the website, that's pretty crap! 

You cannot top up on the website.

You cannot view your usage on the website.

I've put in my request to go back to Utilita tonight, I had no issues with them for the last 4 years.
insight: Multiple critical failures across app functionality, website features, and customer support have left the user unable to manage their prepayment account effectively.

author: NICOLA HUDSON
body: Advisor was polite and helpful. Payments offered were high which wasn't helpful, no prepaid meter option even though this was given as an option if enforcement team visited which seems contradictory
insight: Customer appreciated polite service but expressed concern about inconsistent payment options and contradictory information regarding prepaid meter availability.

author: Tracy
body: exceptional *****
insight: User expresses exceptional satisfaction with the app or service but provides no specific details about what they experienced.

author: Scilla Shea
body: Im glad I changed but I was told I would get weekends half price and havent had anything in writing.
insight: Customer switched providers based on a verbal promise of weekend discounts but received no written confirmation of the offer.

author: Sandra Hawes
body: I am having trouble trying to ALTER to choose own 8 x 1hr slots for half price electricity now, submitting by the 25th of each month for the next month, and/or beyond.  I get error message constantly, so was unable to amend as wanted 🤔🥺🤷‍♀️
insight: User cannot modify their electricity tariff slots due to persistent error messages in the app, preventing them from accessing a half-price electricity scheme.

author: Mark Tritton
body: Easy & quick to use, saves having to phone readings through.
insight: User appreciates the app's convenience for submitting meter readings without needing to contact customer service.

author: CAROL BROWN
body: cant login not even with my finger print. Doesnt recognise my account number and email address even though I have just had a email from them to say my ballance is low. Chat is a waste of time as its a bot. Nothing but hassle. Reset my password and 4 links later its saying all links have been used already. WASTE OF TIME. Don't have a card to top up so dont know what to do tried ringing cant get threw.
insight: User is unable to access their account due to login and authentication failures, and cannot get adequate support through chat or phone channels.

author: Irene Court
body: I contacted Scottish power regarding my direct debit. I have had nothing  but hassle from moving from one property to another. I’ve had numerous people calling me about 4 times a day regarding payment of final bill of old property. Someone was supposed to call me back yesterday but I’m still waiting. Not happy and I get people who can’t understand me on the phone as they are foreign and I can’t understand them so makes calls even more frustrating when speaking.
insight: Customer experienced repeated unwanted calls about final bill payments and poor communication quality due to language barriers when switching properties.

author: Jill Gardiner
body: I'm reasonabley satisfied. Would be nice if the numbers are lower like before covid. And I had a smart meter reader I could see and understand.
insight: Customer accepts current service but wants lower bills and improved smart meter data visibility.

author: Brer Nigam
body: Does the work.. happy with it
insight: User is satisfied with the app's basic functionality and performance.

author: Jayne Furey
body: its made my life so much easier to top up and keep track of what I'm using
insight: User appreciates the app's convenience for managing prepayment top-ups and monitoring energy consumption.

author: Nick Cook
body: Works fine...No issues.
insight: User is satisfied with the app's overall functionality and stability.

author: Nicholas Francis
body: Why doesn't the app give any Android Notification when any of the Power Saver Scheduled Periods or Live Events occur? It means I always miss them!
insight: User is frustrated that the app fails to send Android notifications for Power Saver scheduled periods and live events, causing them to miss important alerts.

author: Beverly Scarff
body: useless
insight: User expresses severe dissatisfaction with the app but provides insufficient detail to identify the specific underlying issue.

author: Kimberliee Peters
body: it has all of a sudden stopped letting me log on to this app and I have no gas at all and have been cut off! I tried to call the company and the line cut me off saying can't help.
insight: Customer lost gas supply and cannot access the app to resolve the issue, with additional frustration from being disconnected during customer support calls.

author: Anonymous
body:
She was full of information, but she spoke too quickly and I felt she was high pressure selling.  I have decided to make use of the 14day period in which you can back out.
I hope you will re-train your employee so that she speaks more slowly and doesn't make customers feel pressurised into buying.
I am still waiting for the confirmation, so that I can make use of the 14 day period in which we can back out.
insight: Customer felt pressured by aggressive sales tactics and rapid speech during interaction, despite 4-star rating likely reflecting the 14-day cooling-off period as a mitigating factor.

author: ampelio
body: it was quick and easy to change the tariff to a new deal
insight: User successfully completed a tariff change quickly and easily through the app.

author: Charlene
body: Had nothing but issues with Scottish power !
insight: Customer has experienced multiple unspecified problems with ScottishPower's service.

author: Denise Bannon
body: Unfortunately, the app is not working. I am unable to login, cannot check balance or credit account and I'm just being sent around in an eternal endless loop. Cache and settings all cleared. Latest app used but still cannot log in, as other users have also reported.
insight: User is unable to access core app functionality including login and balance checking despite troubleshooting attempts, with the issue affecting multiple users.

author: Mr . Pearson
body: I've been using Scottish Power for 15+ years. I find them a good reliable supplier. The only issues I've had has been customer service contact. This doesn't go smoothly sometimes, better in recent years with things like webchat when you get passed the bot.
insight: Long-term customer appreciates reliability but notes that customer service contact channels have been inconsistent, though recently improved through digital options like webchat.

author: Michael H
body: Finally got an email to  get in touch
insight: User is satisfied after finally receiving a response from customer service via email.

author: Gianni S.
body: Good for me.
insight: User expresses satisfaction with the app, though provides minimal detail about what works well for them.

author: Carolina Vika
body: Customer service was really satisfied.
insight: User expresses satisfaction with the customer service experience provided by ScottishPower.

author: Malcolm Pratt
body: Advisor was more than willing to listen and help.
insight: Customer received attentive and helpful support from an advisor.

author: Anita Johnson
body: I been with you some years and you have almost got it right still room for improvement
insight: Long-term customer is satisfied overall but identifies room for service improvements.

author: Christopher Layton Blackham
body:
If i could opt for no 
star i would. I have never dealt with a more impersonal, inept company. I have had a complaint o/s fo 6 months and although SP owe me over £2,000 which they do not dispute they can’t get this paid to me!!!!
insight: Customer has an unresolved complaint spanning 6 months with a disputed £2,000 refund that the company acknowledges but fails to process.

author: Jane
body: The man on phone was very helpful but he needs not to repeat him self more than 2 times  thanks for helping
insight: Customer appreciated the phone representative's helpfulness but found his repetitive explanations unnecessary.

author: Anonymous
body: Good so far . Everything’s works and customer service have been in contact to make sure all is ok .
insight: User is satisfied with app functionality and proactive customer service support.

author: JEAN RUSSELL
body: Pleasant assistant on thephone
insight: User had a positive experience with phone support staff who were pleasant and helpful.

author: MARIAN SAVVA - ANDREOU
body: Never sure if I’m on best rate. Hate having to do everything on line 😡
insight: Customer is uncertain about their tariff and frustrated by the requirement to manage their account entirely online.

author: Mr Archibald
body: There used to be a function to take pictures of your meter reading, where has this gone? It was very handy
insight: User is frustrated that a previously useful meter reading photo feature has been removed from the app.

author: Mr John Marsh
body: Personelcontact exelent
insight: User praised the quality of personal contact and customer service interaction.

author: William Hamer
body: Found the meter reading  box which sits in the house didn't work from the beginning, customer service where very unhelpful and couldn't explain a reason or give any advice on how to get a replacement other then that all works well
insight: Smart meter device failed from the start and customer service provided no helpful support or replacement options.

author: isabella elanko
body: Good still waiting fir the call to fix the problem
insight: User is satisfied with the app but frustrated by delayed response to their reported problem.

author: Mr James Hay-Barr
body: I am experiencing high night rate usage despite only using a fridge freezer and a TV. Is the heat exchanger causing this?
insight: Customer is concerned about unexpectedly high night rate charges and suspects a technical fault with their heating system or meter.

author: Sharon Pinder
body: Never had a problem with Scottish power until now. They have not only taken 2 hours off the 50% weekend electricity but now you have to go into your account every month to tell them what hour you want on what days . I chose to keep mine next month at  weekends but  one week it starts at 12 and ends at the final week in the month at 3/4 who wants to do their washing that late in the day . Not only that who wants to keep logging in  and keep changing day and times ? I have better things to do like look for another utility company! Re reply Have no idea what you are talking about confused reply!
insight: Customer is frustrated with changes to weekend electricity pricing scheme requiring monthly manual account updates with inconvenient time slots, and feels unsupported by unclear company responses.

author: Sharon Y
body: Called up with a problem topping up my smart meter sorted it in 10 minutes thank you
insight: Customer praised the quick and effective resolution of their smart meter top-up issue by customer service.

author: Gillian Patricia Tayler
body: Getting someone to speak is a no no!
insight: User is frustrated with difficulty reaching customer support representatives through the app.

author: Tom Telfer
body: Helpfull and frendly,easy to use
insight: User praises the app for being helpful, friendly and user-friendly despite giving a 4-star rating.

author: Pammy Louise Smith
body: so i found a way to log back in from seeimg the reviews that people cant log back in u have to uninstall the app and re log back in and it worked fine for me
insight: User discovered a workaround for login issues by uninstalling and reinstalling the app, which resolved their problem.

author: DAVID FRENCH
body:
Terrible company complaint from 7th march ESTIMATED BILL £580
STAY AWAY
insight: Customer is extremely dissatisfied with an unresolved billing complaint from March and a disputed estimated bill of £580.

author: Steven
body: All of a sudden I can't login to the app. I've tried to reset my passport, I keep getting a "sorry this link has already been used" My password is correct, I can log into the website with it, I just can't access my account on the app whatsoever. "Oops something went wrong". Please don't reply with generic "contact us" messages. If you check other reviews this is a common problem for multiple customers. I now have to call Scottish power to make payments to my smart meter.
insight: User is unable to access their account via the app due to a persistent authentication bug affecting multiple customers, forcing them to use alternative payment methods.

author: Mrs Hetherington
body: I have been trying to get a refund on my account as I’m over £800 in credit at it’s being nothing but hassle. At the end of the day it’s MY money and not there’s. Now they are saying I have to wait another 22days ( why) I already read my meters and got my bill and there saying there is an error so it will take 22 days to fix yeah right, if I owed you money it would be a different scenario
insight: Customer is frustrated by delays in processing a £800+ credit refund and perceives unfair treatment compared to how the company would handle a customer debt.

author: Ms Nicola Brown
body: Nothing was resolved after 4 weeks and three phone calls I still have no idea if my electricity smart meter is working. I have had to do everything manually on line including asking for a refund of over £300 as my account was so in credit. Customer service representatives communications were poor, with both attitude and language barriers , they had no idea of previous conversations making it very frustrating.
insight: Customer experienced prolonged unresolved smart meter issues compounded by poor communication, inconsistent support interactions, and difficulty obtaining account credit refund over four weeks.

author: Jan
body: My electricity smart meter wasn’t compatible with my gas smart meter. It took the  4 months approximately to replace my electricity meter and I’m know delighted with the outcome. They do email you every week to keep you informed which is good but one of them tried to tell me it was working and I just couldn’t see it!!!!
insight: Customer experienced a 4-month delay replacing an incompatible electricity smart meter and was given misleading information by staff, though weekly email updates were appreciated.

author: Sarah Mcknight
body: I can not log so there for can not able to see the money I have on my meter or top up it a joke I contact the ombudsman and I'm not wasting my time trying to sort out why nobody can asses the app or logs them out
insight: User is unable to log into the app to access meter balance and top-up functionality, and is frustrated with unresponsive customer support.

author: Manuela D
body: It worked OK in till now, I can't get in the App and I need to top up, my credit is running out, I clean catch, I rest the app, nothing, "ups something went wrong" this is the message.
insight: User is unable to access the app to top up their prepaid credit due to repeated login failures and app crashes.

author: Nicola Dyson
body: Scottish Power closed my account 2 months ago without instruction or reason.  I am still battling to get my account opened again, after hours of telephone calls. Further, they have registered my account to some else and sent the bill to my address in the name of another person.  A new account has now been opened for the gas but, despite sending evidence that I am still in the property, I do not have an account for my electricity and nor am I able to pay it.  I am very disappinted after 15 years of really good service.
insight: Customer's account was unexpectedly closed, transferred to another person's name, and has been unable to resolve the issue or regain electricity access despite extensive contact attempts over two months.

author: elisabeth sheehan
body:
When I click on ‘confirmation of your new energy tariff’ in my In Box the envelope icon circle goes round for a few seconds and then goes back to the envelope and I can’t access my new energy tariff. I tried to phone but your lines were busy. I would be really grateful if you could send me a paper confirmation by letter. I am 82 and would really appreciate it. Account 16040211190
Thank you
insight: User cannot access their new energy tariff document in the app due to a technical glitch, and is unable to reach customer service by phone to resolve the issue.

author: Alexa Wren
body: After being swayed by a sales agent, my wife and I switched to Scottish Power. The sales agent looked at our current usage and promised we could pay less with Scottish Power. We switched on 3rd December and received our first bill on the 14th. We were shocked to see that they were charging over £200 for 10 days worth of energy. We made an enquiry and got nothing back until next months bill where they were trying to tell us we’d used over 2000kWh for a month, that’s more than we used in the previous year altogether! We complained and asked this to be looked into, only to be met with false promise of call backs, misdirection of what the problem was, and what felt like patronising blame on the last phone call. They are insisting that we’re now £2k in debt in the space of 3 months and no one seems to be willing to even investigate the possibility that their calculations are incorrect! It’s a 2 person household where we both work in an office 5 days a week, it’s impossible to have used that much energy! I wish we’d never switched and will be switching back to our old provider as soon as our complaint is closed. We’ve already gone to the ombudsman, hopefully they can help!
insight: Customer disputes significantly inflated bills and usage calculations after switching, with poor complaint handling and lack of investigation from ScottishPower.

author: Movy

author: Terry Crawford
body:
I have had the displeasure of being a customer of Scottish Power since 14 January, and can honestly say I have seen some of the worst administrational processes, harassment, and mis-management I have experienced as a customer. 
As the face of Scottish Power, the call centre really do not represent this company at all well (but read on). I have had 6 calls with the customer service advisors since 27 February to dispute an 'estimated' bill (for an unoccupied property. The bill is 2-times greater than my own home bill with a family of 4).  
I have been effectively misinformed twice about how the bill has been derived, and that my request to be escalated to the complaints department (on 6 March) has been actioned.
During my lengthy call on 6 March with the billing department, the operator was unable to explain how the bill had been calculated.  Additionally, I was initially told that the final meter reading had been physically provided by the tenant to then be told it had been automatically generated (read on). A hardline approach was adopted to try and get me to pay, which I resisted, and I was informed that, despite my complaint, I would continue to receive calls about the bill, and to ignore this contact.  It was stated, it would take 7-10 working days to hear from the compliant's team.
Since 6 March, I have received 31 calls and 11 text messages from Scottish Power.  Following their advice, I ignored most calls and messages, but when I did pick up (mainly out of frustration), without checking my account, the advisor went straight into their scripts requesting me to pay the bill.  Hugely intrusive and annoying.
The icing on the cake, was receiving an email last night threatening me with bailiff action.
Whilst typing this review, I have been on the phone to Scottish Power for 85 minutes (on hold for 40 minutes) in an effort to resolve the matter. I have been informed that my complaint that was lodged on 6 March actually hasn't been lodged, so I need to wait a further 7-10-working days to to hear back.  To stop and calls, texts, and threat of legal action, I have requested that my billing process is put of hold until I hear from the complaints team. I was initially informed that they do not have the ability to do this. Ridiculous right? You dispute an estimated bill, which is grossly incorrect, but you are still expected to pay it in the interim whilst the complaints team contact you. Bullyboy tactics in my opinion.
Having spoken to the Supervisor, Naveed, who has agreed to pause the billing process until my complaint is resolved, and cease all contact from the billing team, he was excellent, and his colleague Inga was lovely.  I wish I had spoken with both these individuals at the start of the process. During this call, it was confirmed that the final meter reading was physically provided by the tenant, contrary to one conversation on 6 March. I have been informed that my complaint has now been lodged and I will hear from them in 7-10 days (give or take).
I look forward to hearing from the complaints department in the hope that this horrendous situation can be resolved. 
For directors reading this review, please listen to the call recordings and you will understand my angst.  Happy to discuss your processes and customer service, which are hugely inefficient.
insight: Customer experienced severe administrative failures including incorrect estimated billing, inadequate complaint handling, excessive contact harassment, and only partial resolution after escalation to supervisors.

And no explanation how i can go from £122 account credit too minus £18 then in credit by £22 next day and no reply to my email from them at all. No support,No empathy,No communication. Use another company. ..updated finally got a reply too my email..they stated the phone call was too resolve the problem. The phone call resolved nothing at all with no answers of how a signal glitch only can stop a topup reaching the meter and why my cash was frozen in bank where I couldn't get it back due too their nonsense. Their reply by email had nothing too do with my request just more nonsense about how too use a prepayment meter topup..pathetic non existent support again!!! author: Transform Technology LImited
body:
Communicating with Scottish power is HOPELESS! 
Their overseas? 'Help centre' is USELESS!

I tried for months to get any sense out of Scottish Power. 

In the end, I asked our local MP to intervene -- which he did and we finally gor a human to talk to!

The Problem: Our SMART meter didn't work. 

I raised a formal complaint. 

After weeks of waiting and missed appointments, they finally got a chap to come and add the 'Telemetry' unit to the so-called SMART meter -- on the second attempt as the first time, he didn't have the authority to add the unit, despite having them in his van! -- so had to go away and we had to wait another week for him to return to add the 4G telemetry device. 

After a further 2 weeks we all concluded it still didn't work!

Another Technician came and replaced everything -- SMART meter and Telemetry unit. This time it worked.

Lead time was around 2 months to get to this point.

We have a Heat Pump and wanted a reduced cost Heat pump tariff -- only to be told this week (24th March, now the SMART meter is working) that all heat pump tariffs have been withdrawn!
So the only option was to 'fix for 12 months.'
 
Goodness knows if and when they will introduce another Heat Pump Tariff.

The one glimmer of hope with Scottish Power is that once I raised a Formal Complaint and got a named complaint handler, communications improved and the problem of the SMART meter was resolved -- eventually!

Without the Customer going out of thier way to achive communication, Scottish Power's Customer Service is as close to 'Useless' as it is possible to get!

No one takes any Resposnsibility to solve the Customer's problem! You get passed from one unknowledgable person to the next -- until you put the phone down in frustration!

They don't have enough English speaking staff with sufficient understanding to be able to deal with a problem!

The on-line chat it worthless.

In truth, they don't appear to have enough staff and their 'on the road' contractor initally gave us a lead time of 6 months to change the SMART meter!
In reality the timescale was 2 months -- but only after I kept pushing them for a sensible date!

Until you raise a Formal Complaint, Scottish Power believe the problem is YOU!

We should not have to put up with such appalling service.
insight: Severe customer service failures in resolving smart meter issues, requiring escalation to MP intervention, combined with withdrawal of heat pump tariffs after months of struggle.

The latest update we were given is that we should have received a response from the “back office” within 14 days. The call handler then went on to say, and I quote, “our back office team are rubbish.” If your own staff are saying that, what confidence are customers supposed to have?

If this isn’t resolved urgently, I’ll have no choice but to escalate this to Ofgem and the Energy Ombudsman.

author: Kerry
body:
Title: Business account not set up – left with charges and no control

We are a business operating from 32 The Shambles, Worcester, and our experience with ScottishPower has been extremely poor.

From the start, we notified them that we had taken over the premises, provided full company details and meter readings, and requested an account to be set up correctly in our company name.

Despite this, ScottishPower failed to set up the account properly and refused to accept payments. This left us in a position where we were actively using electricity but had no account, no agreed tariff, and no way to manage or reduce any charges.

Over a period of approximately three months, charges were allowed to build up while we had no control over the situation. We were also unable to switch supplier during this time.

To make matters worse, debt collection correspondence has been sent relating to a previous occupier of the premises, which we have no connection to. We have had to formally dispute this.

We consider this completely unfair. If the account had been set up correctly from day one, we would have been able to manage the account, make payments, and choose an appropriate supplier.

This issue has now been formally raised as a complaint and escalated further.

Based on our experience, the handling of business accounts, particularly change of occupancy, needs significant improvement. Customers should not be left without control of their account while charges continue to accumulate.
insight: ScottishPower failed to set up a business account correctly after change of occupancy, leaving the customer without control while charges accumulated over three months.

author: Douglas Cobb
body:
I switched to ScottishPower from EDF not long ago, and it has been the biggest mistake of my life. Since the switch, I have been harassed relentlessly—text messages, emails, phone calls—nonstop. If it’s not a text, it’s a call; if it’s not a call, it’s a letter. I have never encountered such aggressive payment chasing in my life. Constant calls, even from call centers abroad, all demanding payment for a trivial amount—around £100 for gas and electricity. I have mental health issues, and this constant bombardment has been absolutely unbearable.

In a bid to end this harassment, I spent over an hour on the phone to switch to prepayment—just to settle the small balance. But after all that effort, they admitted they couldn’t switch my gas supply, because their smart meter doesn’t detect it. Now, I’m left with a messed-up system—50% prepay, 50% direct debit—complete chaos. I asked for a technician to check the meter, but was told there are no appointments. They don’t care. They are in breach of regulations and they know it. This company has been an absolute nightmare. Do not—under any circumstances—switch to ScottishPower. You will regret it.
insight: Customer experienced aggressive payment collection, failed smart meter installation, and inadequate support after switching, resulting in a hybrid payment system and unresolved technical issues.

body: very convenient to make payments, check your balance, change tariff, submit meter reading, and read on how to save energy.
insight: User appreciates the app's convenient features for payments, balance checks, tariff changes, meter readings, and energy saving guidance.

author: Mr Burrows
body: 24/03/26 - Yet again another issue with the Android app forcing me into a never ending  cycle. Luckily my work phone is apple and managed to log on but many wont be so fortunate
insight: Android app has a persistent login bug that creates an endless loop, forcing users to seek alternative access methods.

author: Marlene Blake
body: it's a really good company I've had no problems with them.
insight: Customer expresses overall satisfaction with ScottishPower's service and reports no issues with their experience.

author: hywel williams
body: I have been with SP for over 6 years and they have been OK. The people in customer services are always good when you get through. What made me switch was the fact that they kept hassling me to get a smart meter. I was happy to have one, but like 1000s of others in the  UK, the phone signal near the meter is too weak, so their engineer said it was not possible. But that did not stop the constant calls - I suspect it was from a linked company that was paid on achieving a switch target. Never mind how many times I told them that their engineer said it was not possible, the calls kept coming - just lazy automated lack of corporate memory. So 10/10 for the really nice customer services managers, 2/10 for the hidden managers who made a decision to allow others to badger customers for something that was not possible
insight: Customer left due to persistent unwanted contact about smart meter installation despite engineer confirming it was technically impossible at their location.

author: David Lovell
body: My boiler of 50 years was working fine until some jobsworth told the engineer it's out of date and it got to be condemned whilst fitting a new main via through the old systems. I've been taught how to maintain that system for 20 years from a person with 20 years experience maintaining that boiler? Then some guy tells me I've been cut off. you suck. my boiler of 30 years was condemned after working fine for
insight: Customer is angry that an engineer condemned their functioning boiler during a routine job and was subsequently disconnected from supply.

author: Mike Hutchinson
body: The interface to Scottish power is secure, simple and convenient to use.
insight: User appreciates the app's secure, simple and convenient interface design for managing their account.

author: Stephen Lidford
body:
The person who answered the phone was polite and tuck notes about our problems with our very high energy use!!! Asked me to send a photo of our meter which we did, and said he would ring today to sort out our problems!!! He promised he would ring
At 12.30pm today and we have still had no call!
insight: Customer service representative failed to follow through on promised callback to resolve high energy usage concerns, damaging trust despite initial positive interaction.

author: GarethBrowne
body:
Every single consumer in the UK Do Not become a customer of Scottish Power.
They're incompetent, they manufacture fraudulent gas & electricity bills and then sell the fraudulent debt to a company called " Jefferson's".
I used to be a customer 10 years ago in a council flat run by Hounslow Homes.
In that flat 48 Sandy Drive Bedfont TW18 8BE I paid for my electric supply with a pre-paid key meter and the " Heating" was included in my Rent to Hounslow Homes.
Now through the fraudulent actions of a useless, criminal Scottish Power they have illegally put a £3000 gas & electricity bill on my head. They have washed their grubby little greedy and fraudulent hands of me by passing the buck. They could easily remove this fraudulent debt but they choose to immoral criminals. Do Not become a Scottish Power customer and for any current Scottish Power customer's take my advice and leave them before they do this to you. I have already spoken to the Financial Conduct Authority about their actions.
insight: Customer alleges ScottishPower fraudulently billed them for gas and electricity at a property where heating was included in rent, then sold the debt to a collections agency.

author: heather reardon
body: never works can't log into my account forever having problems. Had to re regiyand re download the app worked once now won't work again.

author: Mrs P Hodgkiss
body: I tried to change the times of my  half price electricity and it kept telling me the email you had sent had already been used! So I couldn’t get any further on the app!
insight: User unable to modify half-price electricity time settings due to an app error claiming the verification email was already used.

author: Henry
body: Since swapping providers I can safely say the whole process has been a nightmare

author: Pauline Smith
body:
Totally incompetent complaints handler. Been having issues with our electricity bill since we got an EV charger  installed via Scottish Power on 8th Jan. Our complaint was closed without a resolution. Still waiting on this useless company to deal with our complaint, 10 weeks of phone calls to no avail. Numerous phone calls made and time waisted on this issue. 


Edit * Here is the update you promised. Saying nothing that hasn't been said before. Note that our complaint has now been reopened after your agent closed it without a resolution. 

Dear Mrs Smith,

Complaint number: 

I hope this email finds you well. Please be advised that I am still actively addressing your complaint. The matter has been escalated to the relevant department, and I am currently awaiting their response. I sincerely appreciate your patience and cooperation during this process. Rest assured, I will update you as soon as I receive any information regarding your concerns. Should you have any further questions in the meantime, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Please reply to this email using the same subject line. This will ensure your response is sent directly to me, so I can resolve your complaint as quickly as possible.

Thanks in advance for your help.

Kind regards
KHALIPHA
ScottishPower Customer Care
insight: Customer's complaint about EV charger installation billing issues has been mishandled with poor communication and a premature closure, leaving the problem unresolved after 10 weeks.

author: Martin Evans

Having requested the refund I then noticed my DD had then reduced from the previous £95 to £47. I queried this and was not given a sensible answer.

author: David Jay
body:
5 Stars for the salesman. Very professional.
Following this I have to say I thought the service has been poor. I have received 2 requests for meter readings with link to submit on line . This didn’t work so tried automated telephone and gave readings which didn’t work and I was then put through to an actual person.
He gave me instructions that were pretty much the opposite to that I had received. For instance told me I should advise British Gas of my readings on 24th March and they would then advise Scottish Power. Told me 14 day cooling off period was from 24th. 
At my insistence he eventually put my readings on record.
insight: Customer experienced poor service and conflicting instructions from representatives regarding meter reading submission and cooling-off period, despite initial positive sales interaction.

author: Keith Follett
body: 1 star is too many. I would have given zero but there was not an option to do so. I have been trying to get my smart meter replaced for 2 years. For most of that time I was unable to switch tariffs, have suffered numerous appointment cancellations, had a complaint cancelled and compensations promised that never materialised. I have been paying more than I should for 2 years. Staff dealing with my case have left the company during its extended duration. After my latest cancelled engineering appointment, the complaints team arranged an appointment to discuss what had happened with me. Then they cancelled the appointment 17 minutes after it was supposed to have happened. You can't make it up.
insight: Customer has experienced a two-year failure to replace a faulty smart meter, resulting in billing overcharges, multiple appointment cancellations, broken promises of compensation, and poor complaint handling.

author: Chris B
body:
Scottish Power as far as I'm aware have still done nothing to reduce or eradicate standing charges. I believe standing charges are unfair and extortionate. However much I try to reduce my usage I still face a bill of approximately £400 per year if I used absolutely no gas or electricity.
Follow on: Some companies offer slightly higher unit charges with low or no fixed standing charges. The costs you are talking about should be integrated into the tariffs. As far as I am concerned there is no reason why Scottish Power cannot go down this route  As I said extortionate standing charges.
insight: Customer strongly objects to ScottishPower's standing charges as unfair and extortionate, arguing the company should offer higher unit rates with lower or no fixed charges like competitors do.

After a year of trying to get our smart meter to work correctly Scottish Power has been unable to succeed in getting the electric reading to go through to Scottish Power.

author: Clive Pritchard
body: As an 84 year old with limited IT skills, I found the experience frustrating and annoying. You seem incapable of giving an answer to anything on your webchat, with all other avenues cluttered with stuff I hadn't asked about. To me it was a totally user unfriendly, other than the lady I finally spoke with. She was both charming and informative.
insight: Digital interface and webchat are difficult for elderly users with limited IT skills, though direct human support provided excellent service.

author: H.B.M MONEY
body:
I would give zero stars if I could. I moved into a newly converted studio flat on December 23rd, 2025, and my experience with Scottish Power has been nothing short of a documented nightmare.
 
When I called to register my account and provide my opening meter readings, I was horrified to find that they already had my personal details—but with a completely incorrect date of birth. Worse still, they had me registered as the responsible tenant for ALL 11 studio flats in the entire building!
 
They are trying to charge me for a massive bill dating back to August 2025. During that time, I wasn't even living in North London; I was living in Southwark. Despite me providing clear evidence, including my signed tenancy agreement proving my move-in date was 23rd December 2025, they have been utterly unhelpful.
 
Most concerning is that they refuse to explain how they obtained my personal information, or why they assigned an entire commercial-to-residential conversion to one single person. Their records are a mess, their data handling is suspicious, and their customer service is non-existent.
 
It’s no wonder this company has such a toxic reputation across social media. I am not the only resident in this building disgusted by their incompetence. I am switching suppliers immediately.

 Do yourself a favor and stay far away from Scottish Power. They are a disgrace.
insight: Customer was incorrectly registered for multiple flats with wrong personal details and charged for pre-move-in consumption, with Scottish Power providing inadequate resolution and refusing to explain data handling practices.

author: Simon G
body: Was forced to use the complaints procedure as I could not get a straight answer to a simple question from the fit team. Whoever handled complaint did eventually  get the answer...but i fear even he struggled. Even now I am asking  questio ns of them because nothing was handled properly  in November  2025.  The fit enrolement process is an absolute nightmare to navigate. Even the people who answer the phone don't understand what they are talking about. I was warned by one of your staff that is a nightmare and he wasn't wrong.
insight: Customer experienced prolonged difficulties obtaining clear answers about FIT enrollment, with inadequate support from both phone staff and the formal complaints process.

By the time I had logged in again it said invalid code. I did this at least six times. Then I rang up and eventually got to speak with a human. Was told this option was not up and running yet! Why send a message saying I could choose my hours when I can’t?

I pointed out that it was being reduced by two hours a week and offering, to me, inconvenient times instead.  I understand it but this is not listening to, or helping me by the Company.

author: PAUL WARD
body:
I have been in talks with Scottish Power over the past 6 weeks regarding my electricity account.
Suddenly #, after a regular amount over the past 6 years, my bill suddenly showed I owed £4111.00.
My heart sank. How did this happen?
after weeks of waiting and phone calls, they couldn't explain why this occurred, but instead, told me the bill was correct.
I looked into this myself and straight away, I saw the error.
They did an estimated reading. This was 65kw, that's it, my actual read submitted was roughly 99886kw. So, as you see, a huge error and that was the huge invoice. It was like going back to a new installation, but saying I owed the total amount from 65kw to the reading it is today. I have been with them for years. Terrible customer service, they are not trained and have no way of looking outside the box and investingating. Nobody has taken charge to see this through.


For the people who look into this account and can not see this error straight away, you should be sacked.
The account has been analysed, and still nobody saw this error at first glance.
The stress and worry has been unbearable.
insight: Customer was incorrectly billed £4,111 due to an estimated meter reading error that Scottish Power's support team failed to identify despite multiple investigations, causing significant stress and frustration.

UPDATE:
Still don’t have an account set up… despite the claim below it would be looked into quickly

author: Ian Bagshaw
body:
The company is not proactive and relies on the customer to instigate solutions to problems that should be noticed far more quickly by SP than the customer is able.
I refer to the reduction of a large credit balance by greatly reducing my Direct Debit until the credit balance is virtually gone.  It can be updated by you at any time that you like but it stays unaltered for weeks on end and is hence wrong for much of the time.  This has now been achieved but I had to do all the running.
insight: ScottishPower fails to proactively manage credit balances and account updates, forcing customers to chase solutions that the company should handle automatically.

Full of bugs that are never fixed, basically the opposite of what it was a few years ago.

The final advisor checked the history new terms of contact and set it up for me - result! Maybe review your recruitment and make sure the online forms are accurate
insight: Customer experienced significant difficulty renewing their contract due to poor advisor quality, language barriers, and inaccurate online forms, requiring 10 contacts before receiving adequate help.

body: I have been a loyal customer with this company for decades but very disappointed in the level of support when I applied for £150 winter discount for this year which I haven't received,I'm dyslexic and have anxiety & psychosis alongside several other life long health problems, really thought they'd help me but instead I've been left feeling unsupported and more confused than ever,I'm not fit to work and doubt I'll be able to whatever years I've left,why isn't it how it used to be,ppl on sick were automatically awarded without the confusion of online stuff,which I have no one to help me apart from citizens rights which I cannot attend appointments because my anxietys flared up+left feeling depressed 😔
insight: Long-term customer with disabilities feels unsupported and confused by the online application process for the winter discount scheme, missing the automatic support previously provided.

I've unistalled/reinstalled the app numerous times,re-registered each time,cleared cache daily and restarted my phone after every action, it's sorts the biometrics issue for a few logins then goes back to the original issue of having to login manually.

author: Customer Maria-Chiara
body:
The phone operatives are always friendly and knowledgeable. Whilst they are on the phone to you they go to a great deal of trouble to sort issues out however, in my experience they never fulfill promises to phone back, including when they claim to have made a note in their diary to do so.
In my opinion Scottish Power are incompetent - it took them weeks to  connect my smart meter. After their assurances that I would no longer need to submit meter readings they messaged me to ask me tl do so. Email links they send me never work. I am also very aggrieved that they sent me a  cheque refund for an overpayment they took from me when their operator had given me a choice and I had requested the money be paid back into the account they took it from.
insight: While phone staff are helpful, ScottishPower fails to follow through on promises, has delayed smart meter installation, and made errors in processing refunds.

I do wish to add that the hand display unit has never worked I was quickly handed it and was told it would display maybe overnight and was not physically shown how the meter worked
Poor show for a company I have been with for over 30 years
insight: Customer experienced multiple missed smart meter installations and inadequate compensation despite 30 years of loyalty, with poor support from both the utility and contractor.

When you are able to speak with someone, they are usually friendly and willing to explain things clearly. While the system could be more efficient, the support from the staff makes the experience better.
insight: Staff quality is good but operational inefficiencies like long wait times and tricky processes detract from the overall experience.
