author: Audrey Dodds
body: A bit frustrating as initially representative didn't exactly understand what I wanted answering.  We got there in the end but only to give me another number to call.
insight: Customer experienced poor initial understanding from representative and was transferred rather than having their issue resolved.

author: Leanne
body:
I have had an account with scottish power for 5 years and I needed to remove someones name off the account. The only way to do this apparently was to delete my account and restart, I specifically asked if this would affect my payments because I had managed to get them down to a decent amount and was told no - unsurprisingly when the new account was set up the amounts had changed and risen by £27 a month. I believe this was because I was in credit over £200 with my previous account which of course wouldnt be refunded to me (in case it was needed) until they closed the account and were forced to give the money back. I offered to make an upfront payment but was told it wouldnt bring my payments down to anywhere near the previous amount. 

Very frustrating and unhelpful and will now impact me financially.
insight: Customer was forced to close and recreate their account to remove an authorized user, resulting in unexpected monthly payment increases and loss of credit balance, with unhelpful support staff unable to mitigate the financial impact.

author: Brian
body:
Impossible to contact Scot Pwr by 'phone - tried repeatedly - GAVE UP.
Did it all on-line - reluctantly - I WANTED TO SPEAK TO SOMEONE!
insight: Customer is frustrated by inability to reach phone support and was forced to use the online app despite preferring direct human interaction.

author: Graham Bryce
body:
I didn't like the fact that you changed your sign in requirements without telling those who use your app and internet account what they were and I didn't like your 'chat' function which is no more than an unmanned decision tree that failed utterly to include my problem.

I did like your agent on the phone and the speed with which I got through.  I also liked the fact that she identified my problem and succesfully fixed it. 

In short, your frontline people on the phone are very good.  Your backroom  IT geeks are thoughtless and clearly never think of their ultimate customers when making changes that affect them. This leads to wasted time and frayed nerves.

To be fair, backroom IT geeks tend to behave this way whomever they work for, leaving the poor old phone agent to sort out the mess.
insight: Login process changes were implemented without user notification and the chat support system failed to resolve issues, though phone agents provided excellent service.

author: Tara-yogini
body: This app repeatedly refuses my meter readings and I have to go on live chat to submit them. Kind of defeats the purpose of having an app. Since re loading the app (on advice from agent) it now wont accept my login details till I try to open a new account. Then it logs in. It requires an authentication to log in to the account now but the emails sometimes take more than the 5 mins they allow before they come in. Very frustrating.  Is easier to go online and login to the account without the app. Its a complete waste of time on my iphone.
insight: The app has multiple critical issues including meter reading submission failures, authentication problems, and slow email verification that make it less functional than the web version.

author: P and mrs c PATON
body: Why pester your customers to put smart meters in there property when you know that they won’t work because the signal is not good
insight: Customer is frustrated that ScottishPower actively promotes smart meter installation despite knowing they have poor signal reliability in their area.

author: S!@
body: I find the app incredibly easy to operate. I just inserted the meter reading and received the price for the units I had used immediately. Fantastic app very handy and easy to use 👍
insight: User praises the app's intuitive interface and quick meter reading submission functionality.

author: Gavin Brown
body: I don’t agree with the high standing charges in energy rich Scotland
insight: Customer objects to high standing charges despite Scotland's abundant energy resources.

author: Peter Cordingley
body: impossible to get them on the phone for a refund
insight: Customer is unable to reach the company by phone to process a refund.

author: Jayemgee1973
body: The app has been good so far but suddenly it is impossible to open the meter reading page. I have spoken to someone by chat who suggested deleting and reinstalling but that’s such a hassle. What do I do, just not give readings until sorted and no, I don’t want one of these fancy new meters. I just want a working app
insight: User experiences a critical app malfunction on the meter reading page and is frustrated by the suggested troubleshooting requiring reinstallation, while also expressing resistance to smart meter upgrades.

author: James Rutherford
body:
The system is easy  to review and update tariffs but not easy to make comparisons. I ended up paying a little more than expected for evening rates. Still we will see how it goes if there are any increases due to the present conflict.
Additional: Got a problem now. My biometric sign on has gone and there is no option to bring it back. All that just for changing tariff? Seems wrong to me.
insight: User experienced difficulty comparing tariffs, paid more than expected, and lost biometric login functionality without a recovery option.

author: Caroline Dear
body: Ran up debt because they wouldn’t send an electric bill when we were first transferred despite contacting them often. Smart meters have never worked. Always had to read the meters. As soon as we are clear we will go elsewhere
insight: Customer accumulated debt due to missing bills and non-functional smart meters, despite repeated contact attempts, and plans to switch providers once account is settled.

author: Chris
body: totally horrible .never use this app or company. had issues from the very start they don't care about there customers just want money . any issues or complaints you will get an initial response and then never replied to after that
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with poor customer service, lack of follow-up on complaints, and feeling that the company prioritizes profit over customer care.

author: Mike Binks
body: We have now been waiting for nearly 6 months to have our smart meter repaired with still no idea when this will happen
insight: Customer has been waiting six months for smart meter repair with no communication or timeline from ScottishPower.

author: Beverley Frost
body: Do not like the emails requesting payment day after you received the bill
insight: User is frustrated by aggressive payment reminder emails sent immediately after receiving their bill.

author: Mr Whitehead
body: We the under paid, are getting not a lot for what we pay.
insight: Customer feels they are paying too much for the energy service they receive.

author: Ann Goss
body: The length of time in resolving my complaint was unacceptable. Having first made Scottish Power of the issue of an underpayment on my FIT account in August, and having to raise a complaint in December, the underpayment was finally received the end of February. Although my complaint was acknowledged within a week, I then had to make a call a month later for the complaint to move forward. A complaint handler was assigned to the case but there was no rapport or empathy shown considering I had recently lost my husband. It is a very difficult time for me grieving and I expected my complaint to be handled with more empathy.
insight: Complaint resolution took six months with poor communication and lack of empathy shown by the assigned handler during a vulnerable time of grief.

author: Lesley Aspinall
body: app does not work. my in home display doesnt work so i called customer services amd they told me to use energy insights on the app. my app does not have energy insights i told them this and they told me to uninstall and re install the app when that didnt work they gave me the same advice, then said they didnt know why said the tech department would ring me gave a time and date then once that time had passed i got a note on the app call cancled we have emailed you for more info. i got no email.
insight: User's in-home display malfunctioned and customer service provided ineffective troubleshooting advice, failed to deliver promised technical support, and did not send promised follow-up email.

author: Pamela Ann Montgomery
body: You took an extra £70 out of my account without any warning leaving me short for shopping
insight: Customer was charged an unexpected £70 without prior notification, causing financial hardship.

author: Sean Houlihane
body: Some people might like the 'welcome' call, but for me it was 100% unwelcome. I signed up online (twice, the first attempt just went nowhere for some reason), and had no need at all to speak to someone. I work during the day, and I do not appreciate the 3 minute 21 sec interruption.
insight: User found an unsolicited welcome call disruptive and unwelcome after completing online signup.

author: Michael
body: As with all supply companies just robbery hidden in the contract
insight: Customer believes energy pricing practices are unfair and exploitative, with hidden costs embedded in contract terms.

author: Lesley Irving
body: can not get gas on this app
insight: User is unable to purchase or top up gas credit through the mobile app.

author: Scott Arbuckle
body: keep lowering my Direct debit and I have to constantly go into app and change it back leave it as I want 99% of this apps use is to fix my direct debit
insight: The app repeatedly overrides the user's manually set direct debit amount, forcing constant manual corrections and creating frustration with core app functionality.

author: Stevo Senior
body:
Generally Scottish Power’s performance is good, their App works well & the information provided is helpful in managing electricity & gas usage. 
SP’s negative is that I’ve been waiting 7 months to have my gas meter upgraded to one that is compatible with a Smart Meter.
insight: App and service quality are good but gas meter upgrade delays of 7 months are causing frustration despite positive rating.

author: Viqar
body: keep cutting off
insight: User is experiencing frequent service interruptions with their energy supply.

author: Ken Ord
body: hard to use
insight: User finds the app interface difficult to navigate and use.

author: Debbie Golder
body: does not make sense
insight: User finds the app confusing or unintuitive in its design or functionality.

author: peter talbot
body: what insights app totally does not work
insight: User reports that the Insights app feature is non-functional.

author: Andrew Folley
body: Let’s face it energy prices are not going to get cheaper soon and that’s not down to Scottish Power alone. However, they do like to rack up your monthly payments when you go a few pounds in debit over the winter but seem happy to see your credit increase to hundreds of pounds with no changes when your usage is lower in the Summar. It’s a simple calculation. How much have you spent on energy over the last the last 12 months? Divide that by 12, maybe add 5% for future increases, it’s not hard and their system actually does something similar.  But then they increase it automatically by much more, even though their system says you won’t spend that based on usage. Moral of the story? Don’t trust Scottish Power to get it right, check their calculations and adjust accordingly yourself. You should not have to do this, but Scottish Power obviously want more of your money earning interest for them.
insight: ScottishPower automatically increases monthly payments excessively during winter debit periods while failing to proportionally reduce them during summer credit periods, appearing to profit from customer overpayments.

author: MGF
body: It is now more than 7 months since my meters were changed but SP miscalculated my usage which was less than £1000 over a calendar year and raised my direct debit to £141 per month and did not reduce it despite saying on the phone that the error would be put right. So I am not impressed.
insight: Customer's direct debit was incorrectly increased following meter replacement and was not corrected despite verbal assurance from the company.

author: Lawrence machin
body: really easy to use I would recommend Scottish power
insight: User finds the app intuitive and straightforward to navigate, with a strong recommendation.

author: Carol Street
body: the app so good very easy and quick to use.you can also keep track of your account.
insight: User praises the app for its ease of use, speed, and account tracking capabilities.

author: Fraser rankin
body: I don’t like that it’s hard to speak with some who is uk based but they always try there best
insight: Customer struggles to reach UK-based support staff but acknowledges the team's efforts.

author: shona young
body: Costs are going ridiculous, benefits are reducing like the half price electric hours. The pennies they do in awards make no difference really. Been with them for many many years but less incentives to stay.
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with rising costs and diminishing loyalty rewards, reducing their incentive to remain with the provider despite long tenure.

author: David B
body: Despite there being fixed cost deals available I was not offered one, told to wait a month.  Subsequently got one online with not too much hassle.
insight: Customer was not proactively offered an available fixed cost deal and had to wait before obtaining one through self-service online.

author: Steve Furneaux
body:
Everything takes so long I miss being able to just pick up a phone and talk
Without having to wait on callbacks or the dreadful hold with its little breaks to tell you the same rubbish .
insight: User is frustrated with slow response times and lengthy callback/hold wait processes compared to direct phone support.

author: customer
body: I don’t understand how everything is going here. I don’t know what is negative and positive. Scottish power has
insight: User is confused about their billing information and account details within the app interface.

author: MGM k
body: you have not changed or upgraded it my electric meter to smart meter 2 ,so that it can communicate with gas meter, it's over 2 years now
insight: Customer's smart meter upgrade request has not been fulfilled for over 2 years, preventing gas and electric meter integration.

author: Anne Pinner
body: Still waiting a reply from emails sent.
insight: Customer is frustrated by lack of response to multiple email inquiries.

author: Douglas Maguire
body:
Outrageously cancelled my attempts to move to a fixed tariff, not once but 3 times. Corporate greed gone made. 
Renationalise our utilities now
insight: User's attempts to switch to a fixed tariff were repeatedly cancelled by the app, causing frustration with the company's practices.

author: Customer
body: I think I managed to get a good fixed rate deal, on line. However, the documents received did not reflect the details of the deal I signed up for. Online it said early exit fee was  £50, but the docs stated this was £100 for duel fuel - so misleading details online and unfair comparison with other providers.I queried this on the phone and was told it would be followed up but I have heard nothing. Also no mention of having to phone to sign up for the 50% off w/e deal advertised as part of the deal. How many people think they are getting this automatically but aren't? It was only applied to my deal when I asked for details about it. I may make use of the 14 day cool off period.
insight: Customer discovered discrepancies between online deal terms and official documents, with unresolved follow-up and undisclosed conditions for promotional offers.

author: Doreen Burstow
body: so easy to submit details and edit when necessary
insight: User praises the simplicity and flexibility of submitting and editing account details through the app.

author: Maria Beal
body: Scottish power is good, but I find the standing charge is gastronomical 🤣🤣
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with the high standing charge despite otherwise being satisfied with the service.

author: Drdammie
body:
ReDirects you to the webpage each time..

What’s the point in using an app if it only directs me to the website to enter my login each and every time ? 

I might as well be without the app and visit the webpage myself .
insight: The app lacks standalone functionality and repeatedly redirects users to the website instead of providing a native login experience, defeating its purpose.

author: DEREK Briggs
body: I can't update the app To see my top ups and the meter readings I don't know what's left on meter
insight: User cannot update the app and is unable to view their top-ups and meter readings, leaving them unaware of their remaining credit.

author: Paul Stringer
body: Your operative was very helpful and pleasant buty problem was not resolved.Still waiting for my smart meter to be repaired after 3 years.Pointless having it fitted at all.
insight: Smart meter repair has remained unresolved for 3 years despite helpful customer service interactions, leaving the customer frustrated with the overall utility of the device.

author: Wendy Bellars
body:
I know energy companies have a responsibility to help customers avoid getting into debt, but I rather resent being told to increase my Direct Debit (or else I'll have it done for me) when it's obvious that I'm going to have used more energy in the colder months than I will in the warmer ones ahead.
I also know from experience that if I try to change my Direct Debit to an amount different from that recommended, I'll very probably be charged a one-off fee that obliterates the advantage of being in contol of my own payments.
insight: Customer resents being pressured to increase Direct Debit payments and fears being charged fees for making independent payment adjustments.

author: Mark Hunter's security kcl
body: All 👍
insight: User expresses general satisfaction with the app or service through minimal but enthusiastic feedback.

author: K Baker
body: Always been a loyal SP customer but the way they’ve handled my direct debit account on the last two occasions have been so frustrating when your account as been in credit for a few years now, you receive a refund then they mess about with your direct debit payments 🤷‍♂️
insight: Customer is frustrated with ScottishPower's handling of direct debit adjustments and refund processes despite long-term loyalty.

author: Angela Hope
body: Ive not had the best of experience with Scottish Power, have had bills for people who have never lived at the property. Complaints not really dealt with properly, the only best response has been to say cancelling as have sold property and had final bill and prompt reply! Would I use them again - NO.
insight: Customer experienced incorrect billing for unknown occupants and poor complaint handling, only satisfied when cancelling their account.

author: A Lincs
body: Have had no reason to engage with company other than select tariff online, which works fine. Smart metering should be able to offer more off peak incentives aka economy 7
insight: User finds the tariff selection feature adequate but wants smart metering to provide more off-peak pricing options like Economy 7.

author: Wynter
body: Please don’t trust Scottish Power at all because my son and I had trouble with  them for last 3 years because they broke the law buying sale electricity to my son at 14 years old and Scottish Power can’t take any blame at all instead they blame me and we have told them I’m deaf and they just don’t care at. Scottish Power has been ringing me all time and email me to. I asked nicely for them to speak to my son on behalf and they said no because he not 18 years.
insight: Customer reports ScottishPower sold electricity to a minor without proper consent and failed to accommodate accessibility needs or communicate appropriately with the account holder.

author: Victor Meldrew
body: Stop bullying me to get a smart meter eg in addition to e mails now 3 phone calls 2 in the last 3 days!
insight: Customer feels pressured by aggressive smart meter installation tactics involving excessive phone calls and emails.

author: A Google user
body: too hard to understand
insight: User finds the app interface and functionality confusing and difficult to navigate.

author: Ubaidur Rehman
body: I don't like i leave soon
insight: User expresses dissatisfaction with the service and plans to switch providers.

author: Lynn Anderson
body: my partner us this to pay our gas and eltric bills its very usfull
insight: User finds the app helpful and easy to use for paying gas and electricity bills.

author: William van Vuuren
body: The consultants were great. The problem is with me having cancer and no heating throughout winter due to technical problems with my account and meter which was supposed to be a smart meter replacement years ago. A technician is required to visit my address to fix the problems as a consultant has not been able to do this online.
insight: Customer with serious health needs experienced prolonged heating failure due to unresolved smart meter and account technical issues requiring an in-person technician visit.

author: Ian “Flip” Wall
body: doesn't seem to want open
insight: The app fails to open or load properly for the user.

author: kat mcconnell
body: I don't think that SO offers are the best in the market. Surely, a company as big as SO can put out more generous tariffs. However, the speed of replays is very good.
insight: User appreciates fast customer service response times but finds ScottishPower's tariff offers uncompetitive compared to market alternatives.

author: Geraldine Watson
body: The app is good when it works. I am currently abroad and tried to access my app. I am told to log in like you do on the website. So I enter email and password, then am told to check my email. I do that but the email never arrives. So I am left high and dry unable to access my app check my energy usage while I have been away. Why have an app and treat it like the website? It is most frustrating.
insight: User unable to access app while abroad due to failed email verification during login process, despite app existing as a separate platform from the website.

author: Ronald booth
body: Experience was okay, but could have been a bit clearer, my hearing not great, inquired about a possible scam didn't really get the answers was looking for, told not to worry!
insight: Customer received unclear communication and inadequate reassurance when reporting a potential scam concern.

author: ANDREW FELL
body: Find it difficult to work out my bill
insight: User finds the bill calculation and presentation within the app confusing and difficult to understand.

author: Deniz Onac
body: Could not get into my account to submit readings and pay for my electricity. When I phoned the company , an operator recorded my readings but could not get her computer to calculate my bill. Scottish Power needs to get its IT system sorted out.
insight: User experienced multiple IT system failures preventing account access, bill submission, and calculation, requiring manual operator intervention.

author: Frankfencer
body: it's straight forward.
insight: User finds the app easy to navigate and straightforward to use.

author: Phil Sandey
body: The time it took to resolve the issue and the really poor performance of your finance section allocating funds paid by BACS
insight: User experienced prolonged delays in resolving a billing issue and poor handling of BACS payment allocation by the finance department.

author: stormin norman
body: very good customer service and a nice few bonuses
insight: Customer is satisfied with the quality of customer service and promotional benefits offered.

author: IsabellaI Gordon
body: I have solar panels and have had no feedback on them also can't access any information of kW on my account page.
insight: User with solar panels cannot access generation data or feedback on their account page, limiting visibility into their renewable energy performance.

author: debrah candlish
body: just changed to Scottish power and am very happy with the change
insight: Customer expresses satisfaction with switching to Scottish Power and the overall service experience.

author: gsdnut
body: Everything I need in the app. Scottish Power have great tariffs and targets. Luv it 🩷
insight: User is satisfied with the app's functionality, tariff offerings, and company targets.

author: Jackie Fyfe
body: Unable to fulfill request of refund as couldn’t submit meter readings
insight: User was unable to submit meter readings through the app, which prevented them from requesting a refund.

author: Mark H
body: took 1 star off due to customer support being overly aggressive and quite rude about billing was threatening bailiffs over 200 quid being couple weeks late 😂
insight: Customer experienced aggressive and threatening behaviour from support staff over a minor payment delay of £200.

author: Edward Foden
body:
Too many different operators contacting me. They don't know about other communications.
Too many emails, telephone calls and text messages.
I had to send an email to cease and desist, but I'm still getting them?
If you have mobility problems it's difficult to send back meter readings, especially when the meter is in a awkward place? 
In these circumstances, you should arrange, physical visits by staff to do reading.
insight: Customer is frustrated by excessive and uncoordinated communications from multiple operators and lacks accessible options for submitting meter readings.

author: Paul H
body: Works only intermittently with MG vehicles, won't let you change the charge target and ready time during a session, doesn't support Ohme ePod, and only supports one vehicle per household. Fix these issues and it'll be great.
insight: The app has multiple functionality limitations with EV charging including poor MG vehicle compatibility, inability to modify charge settings mid-session, unsupported hardware, and single-vehicle restriction.

author: Anthony Bell
body: Fast service easy setup
insight: User appreciates the quick service and straightforward setup process but appears only moderately satisfied overall.

author: Doreen.cairns
body:
I tried to see if I’d qualified for the warmer homes but never received very much help when I phoned so I gave up
Scottish power did get back to re this and gave me information to address the problem so TYSM
insight: Customer initially struggled to get help with Warmer Homes scheme inquiry but appreciated ScottishPower's follow-up support in resolving the issue.

author: Jennifer Hardie
body: Last April I requested that a Technician call and tell me what  was wrong with my smart meter because I was repeatedly asked to supply ScottishPower with a reading as my smart meter wasn’t working. He did call very pleasant young man who told me everything was fine and that I should have nothing to worry about going forward.
insight: Customer's smart meter issue was resolved satisfactorily after requesting technician support, though the 3-star rating suggests some residual concern or incomplete satisfaction.

author: Beryl Wishart
body: Being told on bill that cheaper rates were available then phoning in next day to be told all fixed rates withdrawn
insight: Customer was misled about available tariff rates on their bill, only to find fixed rates withdrawn when attempting to switch the next day.

author: Richard Pollard
body:
I left my previous company on the premis from yourselves that you would be cheaper. You have now raised my DD to what it was with Good Energy instead of waiting for the cheaper period in Spring and Summer to affect my balance.

Basically you lied to get me as a customer.
insight: Customer feels misled about promised savings and believes the company prematurely raised their direct debit instead of allowing seasonal rate reductions to naturally lower their balance.

author: MaryWinter
body:
Meter readings for property in question were wrong. 
Scottish Power would not accept my explanation.
insight: Customer's meter reading was recorded incorrectly and ScottishPower refused to acknowledge their explanation or resolve the issue.

author: Acko
body:
You suggested a much higher increase in the monthly direct debit than necessary! On purpose ??? Probably because your quarterly statements aren't quarterly ! My latest only included 2 payments.
Just be consistent. Quarterly should be quarterly.
insight: Customer frustrated by inconsistent quarterly billing cycles and inflated direct debit suggestions that appear intentionally misleading.

author: BARBARA HERRIES
body: The scenario I had when the smart metre switch in oct last year was very upsetting fir me as I was emailing constantly ....times you said I was leaving/moving...I had to get account changed! ...I realise there are many departments but sometimes get different stories of conversations from different people.  Yes it s all sorted but my mental health suffered during this period. Still waiting for thr company to compensate on the trouble it had caused....as they said in an email they would,  but like I say I'm not holding my breath as one department doesn't know what the other has said etc.
insight: Customer experienced poor communication and inconsistent information across departments during smart meter installation, causing emotional distress, and feels let down by lack of promised compensation.

author: Jacob Ellard
body: Getting information about the heat pump tariff was really difficult not just for myself but the energy advisor also.
insight: User experienced significant difficulty obtaining information about heat pump tariffs, with poor support from the energy advisor.

author: Natasha
body: I had to make 4 phone calls for the same thing. Also was told I would receive call back twice, never happened.
insight: Customer experienced poor service quality with multiple phone calls needed and unfulfilled callback promises.

author: Nic
body: Would have rated higher but am having to wait 2 months for a new electricity meter to replace the one that suddenly decided to stop sending readings to Scottish Power (despite our smart meter reading still working!) This means that we can no longer take advantage of the half price electricity slots that we've been using to do the laundry etc.
insight: Customer is unable to use time-of-use electricity tariffs due to a faulty smart meter and a lengthy 2-month replacement wait time.

author: John Wilkinson
body: terrible customer service. Even worse complaint dept. Going to offgem as these think they are above you,talk down to you.hang up on you and basic have no respect for customers
insight: Customer experienced disrespectful treatment from customer service and complaints department staff, including being hung up on.

author: Mackeggie Clare
body: Great app
insight: User is satisfied with the overall quality and functionality of the ScottishPower app.

author: paulina Todswell
body: Scottish power are ok apart from when getting a refund cheque back why doas it take 14 day's to arrive and i just don't understand why they just don't pay you your refund directly to your bank to save on paper instead of a cheque.but no only if you pay by direct debit they will pay you straight into your bank
insight: Customer frustrated with slow cheque refund processing and inconsistent refund methods depending on payment type.

author: Brian Scott
body: Improve access to real customer service personnel. Online managing of the account is good but it doesn't have all the answers. I am living with a temperamental 'smart unit' and cannot get a replacement. Told I am on a list for when they are available. If smart meters are the operator preferred way, why are they not readily available?
insight: Customer frustrated by inability to obtain a replacement smart meter and insufficient access to human support to resolve the issue.

author: Marc
body: Came to our door promising us a lower rate than British Gas for our electricity supposedly saving us £400 per year if we switched. Within our first 2 months our monthly costs had risen dramatically with them increasing our direct debit payments. Will likely cost us a hell of a lot more than promised for an entire year. Regret switching.
insight: Customer feels misled by switching offer as actual costs increased significantly rather than delivering promised £400 annual savings.

author: Andy Gordon
body: Scottish Power appear to be a great power company in general. I really feel they have the customer at the heart of their operations. However, they lack the small technical responsibilities. My IHD has never worked. It has been replaced. I have had a new smart meter. But nothing. My smart meters work but not the IHD. Scottish Power say their app gives relevant info. It does not. It lags by 24 hours so the customer cannot use it to save on energy. There is a problem with HAN but Scottish Power have just dismissed that they can do anything to correct it. This problem has persisted for 5 years!
insight: Despite positive overall impression of the company, a 5-year failure of the In-Home Display and delayed app data has rendered energy monitoring tools unusable for the customer.

author: Consumer
body:
Have had an issue with installation of new smart meter since before Christmas which has only just been resolved. 
Dealt with numerous agents, all very nice , but frustrating to what appeared a simple fix.  instances of case being closed when issue remained on one occasion. Had to escalate to chairman’s office. Issue then resolved very professionally with fair offer for inconvenience / experience received.
insight: Smart meter installation issue took months to resolve despite multiple agent interactions, but was eventually handled professionally after escalation to senior management.

author: Mrs Marsha Stewart
body:
Your advisor when I called was excellent. However, my problem with Scottish power is that I'm a customer at my current address for 42 years and you seem to be changing my tariff with no notifications to me. I am a standard quarterly cash tariff customer, so my bill says. When called your advisor said, when asked, I'm a standard variable rate customer. Result is that after paying my quarterly bill, one month later you estimate my reading and send me a bill. What's that all about.
The only way I can get that fixed every quarter is to call you on a premium number and ask for it to be fixed again.
No matter how good your advisors are, your system is garbage. Please inform the relevant accountant who has decided to improve your cashflow by billing customers out with the tariff specs on their account .
One wee question - are you by chance still losing customers? No wonder!!
insight: Long-term customer frustrated by inconsistent tariff classification and repeated billing errors requiring frequent calls to resolve.

author: Paul Wilson
body: Scottish power still not fixed smart meter communicating with them after being installed few years ago have to input a 20 digit number for gas have complained before but no response except were trying to fix the issue not goo
insight: Smart meter has been non-functional for years with manual workarounds required, and repeated complaints have gone unresolved.

author: Sue
body:
It was busy so the adviser put me on hold several times. The music that was played while I was on hold was TOO LOUD. After approx 25 mins I was put through to someone else, where I was told I would have to wait 28 days before anything could be done. 
Both advisors were very polite and professional.
insight: Long wait times and excessive hold music volume detracted from otherwise professional service, with a 28-day resolution timeframe frustrating the customer.

author: IAN TAIT
body: Logging in aquard.
insight: User is experiencing difficulty logging into the app, describing the process as awkward or cumbersome.

author: Geordie
body: absolutely brilliant app
insight: User expresses strong satisfaction with the overall app experience.

author: Tracy 25
body: Easy online use can see all my info and compare against other months
insight: User appreciates the app's ease of use and the ability to view account information and compare historical billing data.

author: iloveeasyjet
body: Very good website easy to navigate
insight: User appreciates the website's intuitive design and ease of navigation.

author: Tom MCDougall
body: I was phoned and asked if I wanted fixed price tarrif, something I was unsure of, anyway more than the fixed price gets deducted, I feel conned I was told £133 per month, this month was something like £180..i will not be conned like this again, after my year fixed tarrif I will go back to my usual payments
insight: Customer feels misled about fixed-price tariff costs, with actual charges significantly exceeding the quoted £133 monthly amount.

author: Mervyn pollard
body: It took ages to get through then didn't change the tariff like I asked from commercial to residential so had to get back in touch with you to sort it all out
insight: Customer experienced long wait times and the requested tariff change from commercial to residential was not processed correctly, requiring follow-up contact.

author: Ronald Webb
body: Monthly payments decreased by Scottish power as in credit. No communication to inform monthly payments no longer enough to cover monthly usage, account over £300 in debit. Again no communication to say say account in debit equals  a very worried 94 yr old parent. A company with no concerns for customers.
insight: Customer experienced unexpected account debit due to decreased payments without notification, causing significant distress to elderly dependent.

author: Jonathan Pattinson
body: Happy
insight: User is satisfied with the app or service overall.

author: Andy Dickson
body: Great!
insight: User expresses general satisfaction with the app or service.

author: Joyce Carlisle
body: I know have an awful time putting my Meter readings in. I cannot get on line. Can't get the Automated line. I've been with your company for years and its worse now than it's ever been.
insight: Long-standing customer is frustrated with difficulty accessing the online platform and automated systems to submit meter readings.

author: Maria
body: First couple of representatives were not able to assist and the call back request was ignored. However when put through to someone who understood our problem it was quickly resolved
insight: Customer experienced poor initial support but issue was resolved once transferred to a knowledgeable representative.

author: Perry
body: Simple online experience.  Would have been 5 stars but was misinformed about smart meter.
insight: Customer was satisfied with the app's online experience but received incorrect information about smart meter installation or functionality.

author: Mr Colverson
body: Having had a smart meter fitted the reading did not recognise with Scottish power so had a calisen to resolve the reading dispute.  Hopefully my credit is not affected.

author: Lesley Minchin
body:
Web site was not user friendly, it took 6 attempts to get through to an actual agent, signal kept dropping and losing the call. couple of times used the call back facility but that also kept dropping the call once it rang me back as next in the queue. 

Once connected Vimkitha was super friendly and appologised for the issues - she sorted the issues for switching addresses and also sorted out the refund due - well done this agent.
insight: Poor digital and phone support experience with repeated call drops was redeemed by one agent's exceptional service in resolving address switching and refund issues.

author: Darran Broderick
body: I couldn't get through on the phones originally to set up my account after moving in. I ended up setting it up with a chatbot. I had multiple quotes ranging from £96 monthly to £174 and higher. My first bill was £379 which even though I had a direct debit set up I was asked to settle when I called your call center. The lady on the phone wasn't able to confirm when my direct debit was set up and how paying the £379 would affect my Direct Debit payments. Not a great start and due to an error on the system which wouldn't allow me to enter the correct moving in date I was chased for the £14 due from the 22nd January to the 25th January.
insight: Customer experienced multiple issues including difficulty setting up account, unexpectedly high first bill, unclear direct debit arrangements, and subsequent chasing for a small disputed amount.

author: John Forrest

author: Stephen Bedder
body: Just cost me £112 for less than 40 days gas a total rip off of oap who dosent understand how use e mails . As far as Im concerned dont trust them

author: Matthew
body:
I have pre-payment smart meters that before this issue had never even gone into emergency credit, yet for the third year in a row some inexplicable debts have appeared. This happened in September of 2025 and I got in touch twice to ask for an explanation or rectify a mistake but nobody could provide an explanation or show me evidence that I actually owed the debt. Nobody ever got back in touch with me and it took until 3rd March 2026, when I called along with an advisor from CAB to get the issue sorted. As with the previous two years the money was refunded to my meters. It just took so much longer this time and I’m pretty sure that if I hadn’t enlisted the help of CAB I would have been ignored completely. 
So, I’m obviously happy with the outcome of 3rd March but I have to say that the performance of Scottish Power up until then falls far below the standard I would expect. This issue with the unexplained debts took over £60 from my meters between September and November last year, which coincided with another price increase and the onset of winter which has left me in fuel poverty, and the prices just keep increasing. Hence, despite the satisfactory outcome of the date mentioned above this can only ever be a mixed review. To put this in perspective; until the issue occurred I had not used my overdraft for years. Now I have two overdrafts and am pretty much living in them. This is what can happen when your energy supplier can take money from your meter that you don’t owe them and then hope that you don’t pursue the matter; it has a domino effect on the rest of your finances.
insight: Recurring unexplained debits on pre-payment meters went unresolved for months until CAB intervention, causing financial hardship despite eventual refund.

author: Garry Mcintyre
body:
Website difficult for a first time user.
Logging in to account through email link impossible when access code sent to email, went round in circles.
Trying to find easy access to tariffs not easy.
Demands for payment being sent when I thought had set up direct debit over phone.
Computer crash meant had to go through bank details a 2nd time.
Still didn't happen, more messages about paying my debt.
Tried online direct debit, unsuccessful 
Tried chat bot, couldn't get access.
More messages about debt.
Paid a bit off my account.
Managed to get a person to help set up direct debit, they logged off!
Next person was very helpful and eventually got direct debit set up.
Without their help I would be with a different provider now!
All seems to be ok, only niggle being that energy usage doesn't show the 2 rates!
insight: Multiple failed attempts to set up direct debit combined with confusing website navigation and payment demands created frustration, only resolved by exceptional customer service intervention.

author: Hugo  Santos
body:
Unfortunately, communication within your company appears to be seriously lacking.
I have repeatedly informed your team that my property does not have a gas supply. This was first explained when I moved in back in October, and again in December when I received a gas bill on my account. I contacted you immediately, as this issue had already been clarified. Each time, I was met with confusion and asked, “What do you mean your house has oil and not gas?” requiring me to explain the situation again from the beginning.
In January, when my smart meter was due to be installed, the engineer arrived and the same discussion about a non-existent gas meter took place once more. After making several phone calls, he returned and informed me that he would proceed with installing the electricity smart meter but advised me to contact Scottish Power directly, as your records still incorrectly showed that I had a gas meter.
The level of customer service has been extremely disappointing, and the lack of communication between departments is particularly concerning.
Although the issue now appears to be resolved, this experience has significantly impacted my confidence in your service. Once my one-year contract comes to an end, I will be exploring alternative providers, not necessarily for a better price, but for a better standard of service.
insight: Poor internal communication led to repeated failure to correct incorrect gas supply records, damaging customer trust and leading to contract abandonment plans.

author: Wendy Phyllis Read
body: Absolutely disgusting I was scammed so struggled with my bills am disabled so cant get out much, they forced me from direct debit onto pay and go apparently on the 7th and gave me £30 credit, I kept checking the app as you can apparently top up there and it hadnt changed so had no idea I was already on pay as you go meters, yesterday the whole day it was cold my boiler wasnt working, frozen all night then today eventually was told they put me over but not put the £30 on wanted me to go outside to the meter so had to get my mum over to then I rang back to be told they did it on the 7th the £30 had run out but I couldnt check how much credit was left unless I go outside, disgusting treatment and not helpful at all left me to freeze, my apps still not changed for me to be able to top up.
insight: Customer was forcibly switched to pay-as-you-go without proper notification, credit failed to load, app didn't update, and poor support left them unable to heat their home in winter.

author: Trevor
body: I'm not complaining about change of tariff but in 2 years you haven't read my smart meters about 7 to 9 times. I'm disabled and it causes me great pain to read my meters outside my house. It happens way too many times and  no other suppliers have had to do it. I am waiting for a bungalow to come up and won't be using your services because of this.
insight: Disabled customer experiences significant hardship due to repeated failures to read smart meters remotely, forcing manual readings despite accessibility needs.

I feel that I have been duped into changing at a cost.
insight: Customer feels misled by sales regarding switching savings, as the quoted monthly payment of £99 increased to £161 after two payments, resulting in higher costs than their previous provider.

Our experience with Scottish Power over the last few years has been very poor. Long waits to get through on telephone. Longstanding issues with meter gone unresolved with poor communication. Addressing letters our deceased mum despite having been informed of her death. 
Promises of follow up calls repeatedly dishonoured. 
No communication of any action plan regarding meter after 2 visits from engineers.

author: George Harris

Horrendous from day one. Set up my account over the phone. Endless please hold and redirect to different teams. 

Every phone call is the same there seems to be an issue with your account. 

Most recently they phoned to tell me they are putting me on a new tariff and the guy did not select a payment option. So it defaulted to a different method leaving me in negative.

A new tariff to save money resulted in a net loss to the customer and to top it off because of the change I was given a late payment charge plus an administration fee to amend something I had no choice in !!!! 

DO NOT USE

author: PABLO.EL.DIABLO
body:
The worst app I’ve ever used to top up my phone.

It doesn’t show the real account balance — everything is delayed or inaccurate. Information appears with a noticeable lag, so you’re never sure whether your top-up has actually been processed.

There’s no transparency, no real-time updates, and absolutely no sense of control over your own money. Extremely frustrating and unreliable.
insight: Account balance information is delayed and inaccurate, leaving users uncertain whether transactions have been processed.

It’s now been a month and several phone calls and my gas prepayment meter still is not working, it’s not on my app, I have no way of topping up, all I get is empty promises!! So I’m sure the bill is growing but as I have no payment method to pay and the complete incompetence of Scottish power, I am probably going to be gorce to find a real energy supplier!!   Don’t get smart meters with this company couldn’t organised a piss up in a brewery!!!
insight: Smart meter installation failed to complete properly, leaving customer unable to top up their prepayment account and unable to pay bills despite multiple contact attempts with poor support.

The experience has been ok until Scottish Power are questioning my meter reads , I live in a studio flat I rarely use gas other than for bathing.

Due to the reply I've reduced the rating because that response was completely incompetent and did not pay attention.  I sent pictures of my reads and it shut you guys up.

body:
My app has been working well for 10 years - I tried to access it yesterday but my fingerprint ID failed.
It was showing my ID had been compromised so I tried to change my password - many times.   To do this uou have to receive a code from yourselves - 5 mins to put in.   The email was so slow in coming the time expired each time.  In the end I had to phone and was given a ridiculously long password to enter - however the code from yourselves didn’t arrive again.
I gave up.    I will phone in my monthly consumption in future.

On the plus they arent too pushy with the payments I am making and changed my tarrifs a few times succefully bringing the cost of my Gas and Electricity down, hence it's not 1 star review. author: Samuel
body:
Changed to Scottish power from octopus back in October 2024 when a really nice, polite young lad knocked on my door, I thought what the heck, I’ll give him a sale.
Fast forward 12 months I decided to switch back to Octopus, just because they provide more perks, somehow between Scottish power and octopus they only managed to switch my electric, I found this out when a representative from Scottish power rang me to say they would offer me £50 credit if I came back to Scottish power before my cooling off period with octopus ended, annoyed and confused with the situation I agreed.
Fast forward again to 2nd February and my Scottish power account has not been updated. I rang them to be told yeah your on the database as our customer but your account hasn’t been set up, after being passed around 5!!!!! Customer service agents I was told I just need to set the account up, oh and we have no knowledge of the £50 credit we offered you!
In addition to this I was told my meter was a pre-paid meter, which it 100% isn’t but the Scottish power agent was adamant that it was, this was the final straw for me, I’ve switch both my Gas and Electric to EDF who confirmed my meter is not a pre-paid meter, oh and my query with them was sorted with one, clear speaking helpful agent. Scottish Power I don’t know what is wrong and why such a simple request of setting up my electric account is so difficult, seems you couldn’t organise a buffet at a restaurant!
insight: Customer experienced significant account setup failures, poor meter classification, lack of knowledge about promised credits, and frustratingly inefficient customer service across multiple transfers.

author: Stuart
body:
I had no gas supply from early on Saturday morning, and tried contacting SP by phone. The offices were closed at the weekend and the message played advised me to use "chat" on the website. This I tried but to no avail. The answer to any questions I typed was "I have no answer to that question. When I wrote"I have no gas supply " I got the same response...."I have no answer to the question" I then noticed that everytime I typed another query, the panel showed "not sent" in red letters.
I eventually rang the National Gas Emergency Centre (who are available at the weekend) and explained that although it wasn't an emergency, I had no heating and no gas hob to use. The lady was extremely helpful and asked if I had checked my smart meter. This I went and did, only to find that it was dead, and therefore not allowing gas to flow. I would have to wait until 9am on Monday for the phone lines to open at SP. When I eventually got through to an agent, he was extremely polite and helpful. He arranged for an engineer to come out from a smart meter company within 3.5 hours. The engineer pointed out that my smart meters (gas and electric) were Stage 1 units and prone to problems. He was surprised however that the meter battery was completely empty and thus defunct, within a comparitively short period of time. Within 2 hours he had installed a new Stage 2 gas meter and connected a new communications module to the Stage 1 electric meter, so that the readings can be sent to SP. He did however advise me to contact SP agatand arrange for a Stage 2 electricity meter to be installed, where the new communications module can be used, thus allowing both gas and electricity to be sent simultaneously to SP. 
My complaint is the system that SP offer, to notify them of issues, is not fit for purpose, and the Stage 1 smart meters aren't fit for purpose either.
I have no complaint against the agent in Warrington who answered my call, not against the meter engineer who was very helpful and explained everything to me
insight: Customer service communication channels (website chat) are non-functional during emergencies, and Stage 1 smart meters are unreliable and prone to failure.

author: Nic
body:
The lady I spoke to after receiving incorrect correspondence from Scottish Power (again) was very helpful.  As part of the bereavement team, that’s unsurprising, and I’ve found most people on that team to be very understanding on the phone. 
The automated systems, however, are in dire need of a serious overhaul. Failing to use information provided, continuing to chase outstanding bills despite telling us we don’t need to pay yet, sending “sorry you’re leaving us” letters (one for each of gas and electric in the same envelope) when some random (as far as you know) person asks to move the services, and after you yourself have cancelled it, are hopeless errors which are due to shoddy workmanship in the systems. It’s lazy, it’s unacceptable, and frankly most disappointing. Further… when dealing with a bereavement, it can be really quite upsetting. I don’t like being put in a position to have to make additional phone calls to get you to fix problems that should never have arisen if only your systems actually worked.
insight: Automated systems are creating repeated errors in billing and account management that require manual intervention to resolve, causing frustration during sensitive bereavement situations despite positive experiences with human staff.

Consequently, I've not been able to submit readings or review my direct debit payment until I serendipitously discovered that re-registering my  online account (despite already having an existing registratinon) was the key.  I'm resigned now to having to re-register every time I need to access my account.  Just to add insult, non of the fixed rate offers on the account page work and one is just taken in an endless circle of clicking an accept button that does not accept.
